Since 2014, Chicago Fleet Wraps has handled commercial fleet programs for Amazon DSP operators, regional distributors, trade contractors, and national brands. Every install uses Avery Dennison MPI 1105 or 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl — not calendered, not economy.
Call (312) 597-1286 or submit our online form. We respond within 2 hours with detailed pricing based on your vehicle type, fleet size, and coverage.
Our design team creates custom mockups on your exact vehicle template. Unlimited revisions until you're 100% satisfied. Pantone color matching included.
We pick up your vehicles from anywhere in Chicagoland. Professional installation with premium Avery Dennison & 3M vinyl. 2-year workmanship warranty.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a commercial fleet wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Fleet wrap pricing depends on vehicle size and sq-ft coverage. Premium cast vinyl (Avery Dennison MPI 1105 / 3M IJ180-CV3) is $9.75/sqft. Color change wraps are $8.25/sqft. A cargo van full wrap starts around $3,750, Sprinter vans from $4,700, and box trucks from $5,000–$10,900. Fleet discounts: 3% for 2–4 vehicles, 7% for 5–9, 11% for 10–24, 15% for 25+. Use our Instant Wrap Price Calculator for exact pricing on 310+ vehicles.

**Q: How long does a vehicle wrap last?**
A: Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl wraps are rated for 5–7 years of outdoor use. With proper care — hand washing, avoiding brush car washes and high-pressure washers — fleet wraps in Chicago regularly last 6–7 years.

**Q: Are vehicle wraps tax deductible?**
A: Yes. Commercial vehicle wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179 as a business advertising expense. Consult your tax professional for details specific to your situation.

**Q: How long does fleet wrap installation take?**
A: Cargo vans: 1–2 business days. Sprinter vans: 2–3 days. Box trucks (16–22 ft): 2–3 days. Box trucks (24–26 ft): 3–4 days. Fleet orders are phased to minimize vehicle downtime. Free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland.

**Q: What areas does Chicago Fleet Wraps serve?**
A: Chicago Fleet Wraps serves the entire Chicagoland area including Chicago, Schaumburg, Naperville, Aurora, Elgin, Joliet, Evanston, Skokie, Oak Park, and 75+ cities across Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry Counties. Free fleet pickup included.

Chicago Fleet Wraps is Chicago's leading fleet wrap company — designing and installing consistent, professional vehicle graphics across fleets of 3 to 50+ vehicles. We wrap cargo vans, box trucks, sprinter vans, pickup trucks, and specialty vehicles for contractors, service companies, and enterprise fleets. Volume discounts up to 15%. Free pickup and delivery across Chicagoland. 2-year workmanship warranty on every wrap.
Unwrapped vans miss 40,000+ daily impressions in the neighborhoods you already serve. Every unmarked panel is your competitor's free advertisement.
Cheap calendered vinyl fails in 12–18 months in Chicago winters. We use only Avery Dennison MPI 1105 cast vinyl — rated 5–7 years, zero paint damage claims in 10+ years.
Wraps are IRS Section 179 advertising expenses — 100% deductible year one. A $3,750 cargo van wrap costs ~$2,700 after tax at the 28% bracket. Fleet discounts up to 15%.
Any business with multiple vehicles on the road benefits from fleet wraps. Your vehicles are already covering the miles — fleet wraps turn every mile into a marketing impression at just $0.04–$0.07 per thousand views. That's 100x cheaper than Google Ads and 50x cheaper than radio.
Fleet wraps deliver measurable business outcomes that compound over time:
Our fleet rollout process is designed to minimize vehicle downtime while ensuring brand consistency across your entire fleet:
We use only premium cast vinyl rated for Chicago's extreme climate — from -10°F winter lows to 95°F+ summer highs:
Both materials are non-damaging to paint and can be cleanly removed at the end of their life or when you rebrand.
Free estimate response within 2 hours · Free fleet pickup included

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do fleet wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Fleet wrap pricing starts at $3,150 per cargo van. Box trucks start at $4,700. Volume discounts of 5% (3-4 vehicles), 10% (5-9 vehicles), and 15% (10+ vehicles) are available. Fleet wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179.

**Q: Do you offer free fleet pickup in Chicago?**
A: Yes! We offer free fleet pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland — 75+ cities. We come to your location, professionally wrap your vehicles at our 4711 N Lamon Ave facility, and return them to you.

**Q: How long does a fleet wrap take?**
A: Most cargo vans take 2-3 days per vehicle. Box trucks take 3-5 days. We can schedule multiple vehicles simultaneously to minimize fleet downtime. Rush scheduling is available.

**Q: What materials do you use for fleet wraps?**
A: We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl exclusively — never calendered film. Both are rated 5-7 years outdoor durability with a 2-year workmanship warranty included.

Free pickup and delivery across Chicagoland. Call (312) 597-1286 for a free quote.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do vehicle wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Vehicle wrap pricing in Chicago: car full wraps from $2,800, cargo van wraps from $3,150, sprinter vans from $3,700, box trucks from $4,700. Partial wraps start at $1,200. Free quotes — call (312) 597-1286.

**Q: What is the best vehicle wrap company in Chicago?**
A: Chicago Fleet Wraps is Chicago's highest-rated commercial vehicle wrap company with 4.9 stars on Google. We've wrapped 9,400+ vehicles over 24+ years using only Avery Dennison and 3M cast vinyl.

**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last in Chicago?**
A: Vehicle wraps using Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl last 5-7 years. We offer a 2-year workmanship warranty and have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years.

**Q: Can you wrap any vehicle in Chicago?**
A: Yes — we wrap all vehicle types: cargo vans, sprinters, box trucks, pickup trucks, SUVs, cars, electric vehicles, trailers, and full commercial fleets. Single vehicles and fleet programs welcome.

**Q: Do vehicle wraps damage paint?**
A: No! When installed and removed correctly, vehicle wraps protect your factory paint. We use premium cast vinyl and have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years of Chicago area installs.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a cargo van wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Cargo van full wraps start at $3,150. Sprinter vans start at $3,700. Partial wraps start at $1,200. Pricing varies by vehicle size, coverage area, and design complexity. Contact us for a free estimate.

**Q: What vans do you wrap in Chicago?**
A: We wrap all cargo vans including Ford Transit, Mercedes Sprinter, Ram ProMaster, Ford E-Series, Chevy Express, GMC Savana, Nissan NV, and all electric vans. We handle single vans and full fleet programs.

**Q: How long does a van wrap last?**
A: Van wraps using Avery Dennison MPI 1105 or 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl last 5-7 years. All wraps include a 2-year workmanship warranty. We have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years.

**Q: Do you offer free pickup for van wraps in Chicago?**
A: Yes! We offer free vehicle pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland. We come to your business, wrap your vans at our 4711 N Lamon Ave facility, and deliver them back to you.

Free pickup and delivery. 2-year workmanship warranty.
Unwrapped vans miss 40,000+ daily impressions in the neighborhoods you already serve. Every unmarked panel is your competitor's free advertisement.

Full truck wraps cover every visible panel for maximum impact. On a box truck, that means 200+ square feet of billboard-quality advertising space per side. Full wraps generate the most impressions and deliver the best ROI for high-visibility routes.
Partial truck wraps cover strategic panels — sides, tailgate, rear doors — at 40–60% of full wrap cost. Partial wraps are ideal for businesses starting their fleet branding or working with tighter budgets. Learn more about partial wraps â
Commercial truck wraps are the single most cost-effective advertising investment for businesses with trucks on the road:
View full truck wrap portfolio â
Fleet discounts: 5% (3–4 trucks), 10% (5–9), 15% (10+). Full pricing guide â
Pickup truck wraps cost $2,200–$3,800. Box trucks range from $4,200–$10,000 depending on size. Partial wraps start at $1,200. Fleet discounts of 5–15% apply for 3+ trucks.
We wrap all truck types: pickup trucks, box trucks (16–26 ft), flatbed trucks, stake trucks, dump trucks, cement mixers, and specialty commercial vehicles.
Full wraps provide maximum visual impact and advertising space. Partial wraps cover key panels at 40–60% of the cost. For commercial trucks on high-traffic routes, full wraps generate significantly more impressions and ROI.
Our truck wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5–7 years outdoors. Combined with our 2-year installation warranty, your wrap is built for years of commercial use in Chicago weather.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a box truck wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Box truck wraps in Chicago start at $4,700 for 16-20 ft trucks. Larger 22-26 ft trucks start at $6,700. Full wraps cover the cab, sides, and rear. We offer free estimates — call (312) 597-1286.

**Q: What size box trucks do you wrap?**
A: We wrap all box truck sizes from 12 ft to 26 ft, including Isuzu NPR, GMC Savana cutaway, Ford E-Series cutaway, Freightliner MT45, and all major brands. Both cab and box can be fully wrapped.

**Q: How long does a box truck wrap take?**
A: A standard box truck wrap takes 3-5 business days. The vehicle must be clean and any dents should be addressed first. We offer free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland.

**Q: Can you wrap a pickup truck in Chicago?**
A: Yes! We wrap pickup trucks including Ford F-150, F-250, F-350, Chevy Silverado, Ram 1500/2500, and all configurations. Full wraps, partial wraps, and tailgate wraps are all available.

We service all of Chicago and surrounding areas with professional installation, free estimates, and 2-year workmanship warranty.
Whether you're a Chicago business owner seeking brand visibility or managing a multi-vehicle fleet, Chicago Fleet Wraps provides comprehensive wrap solutions tailored to your needs. Our certified team combines cutting-edge design technology with masterful installation techniques to deliver stunning, durable results.
We use exclusively premium materials from Avery Dennison and 3M, rated for 5-7 years of durability. Every installation includes detailed vehicle preparation, precision application, and quality assurance to ensure your investment looks perfect and lasts.
With over 24 years of experience and 9,400+ vehicles wrapped, Chicago Fleet Wraps stands as Chicago's most trusted vehicle wrap provider. Our commitment to excellence spans every project, from single-vehicle wraps to comprehensive fleet programs.
Our team has wrapped everything from single-car owners to large commercial fleets. We specialize in:
Vehicle wraps deliver exceptional ROI compared to traditional advertising. Research shows wrapped vehicles generate 4x more views than static billboards at a fraction of the cost.
Quality wraps using premium materials typically last 5-7 years with proper care. UV exposure, climate, and maintenance affect longevity. We use fade-resistant 3M and Avery Dennison materials rated for long-term outdoor exposure.
Yes. Professional removal by experienced installers ensures zero damage to original paint. We've removed thousands of wraps without incident, protecting your vehicle's resale value.

## EV Wrap Finishes & Materials
## EV Wrap Pricing Guide
## EV-Specific Installation Considerations
## Frequently Asked Questions About EV Wraps
## Commercial EV Fleet Wraps
## Ready to Transform Your Vehicle?
Chicago Fleet Wraps is Chicagos premier specialist for electric vehicle wraps. With 24+ years of experience and 9,400+ vehicles wrapped, we understand the unique body panels, frunk geometry, and paint-protection requirements of Tesla, Rivian, Lucid, and other EVs. Zero verified paint damage claims. 2-year workmanship warranty.
Electric vehicles present unique challenges that generic wrap shops are not equipped to handle. The large, uninterrupted body panels on a Tesla Model 3 or Rivian R1T require seamless vinyl application without visible seams or stretch marks. The frunk (front trunk) lid, charge port door, and camera housings demand precision cutting and heat-forming techniques that only experienced EV wrap specialists can execute correctly.
Chicago Fleet Wraps has wrapped hundreds of Tesla Model 3, Model Y, Model S, Model X, and Cybertruck vehicles, as well as Rivian R1T and R1S, Lucid Air, and commercial EV fleets. Our certified technicians are trained on EV-specific installation techniques, including proper handling of aluminum body panels and camera/sensor areas.
We use exclusively Avery Dennison and 3M cast vinyl for all EV wraps. These materials are rated for 57 years of outdoor exposure and are safe for all EV paint types, including Teslas water-based paint and Rivians aluminum body panels.
Prices vary based on design complexity, material selection, and vehicle condition. Fleet discounts available for 2+ vehicles. Get a free quote
As Chicago businesses transition to electric fleets from Amazon delivery vans to HVAC service vehicles Chicago Fleet Wraps is ready to brand your EV fleet. We offer fleet discounts for 2+ vehicles and can coordinate multi-vehicle wrap programs with consistent branding across your entire fleet.
Fleet discounts: 3% off (24 vehicles) 7% off (59 vehicles) 11% off (1024 vehicles) 15% off (25+ vehicles)
Get your free professional estimate today. Call (312) 597-1286 or submit an online request. We respond within 2 hours with detailed pricing and timeline.
Why wait? Schedule your free consultation now and discover why 9,400+ Chicago businesses trust Chicago Fleet Wraps.
- Charge Port Door: The charge port door is wrapped as a separate panel to ensure clean lines and easy access. We use precision-cut templates for each EV model.
- Frunk (Front Trunk): The frunk lid is wrapped separately with special attention to the unique geometry of each EV model.
- Aluminum Panels: Rivian and other EVs with aluminum body panels require special surface preparation to ensure proper vinyl adhesion without risk of galvanic corrosion.
- Glass Roof Panels: Teslas panoramic glass roof can be wrapped with tinted vinyl for heat reduction and privacy, using automotive-grade window film.
- Paint Compatibility: Teslas water-based paint is fully compatible with cast vinyl wraps. We perform a paint hardness test on all vehicles before installation.
- Amazon Delivery Vans (Rivian EDV): We are experienced with Rivian Electric Delivery Vans used by Amazon and other logistics companies
- Ford E-Transit: Commercial cargo van wraps for trade service businesses

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What is included in your vehicle wrap service?**
A: Our vehicle wrap service includes design consultation, material selection, professional installation, and post-installation care instructions.

**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last?**
A: High-quality vinyl wraps typically last 5-7 years with proper maintenance. We use premium materials from 3M and Avery Dennison.

**Q: Do you offer custom design services?**
A: Yes, we offer complete design services from concept to installation. Our team will work with you to create a design that perfectly represents your brand.

â â â â â 4.9 · 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsHow Much Do Vehicle Wraps Cost in Chicago?
Vehicle wrap costs in Chicago range from $400 for basic lettering to $10,000+ for large box truck full wraps. This guide breaks down pricing by vehicle type, coverage level, and fleet size — with transparent pricing from Chicago Fleet Wraps, Chicagoland's most experienced wrap shop with 9,400+ vehicles completed since 2001.
Unwrapped vans miss 40,000+ daily impressions in the neighborhoods you already serve. Every unmarked panel is your competitor's free advertisement.
Cheap calendered vinyl fails in 12–18 months in Chicago winters. We use only Avery Dennison MPI 1105 cast vinyl — rated 5–7 years, zero paint damage claims in 10+ years.
Wraps are IRS Section 179 advertising expenses — 100% deductible year one. A $3,750 cargo van wrap costs ~$2,700 after tax at the 28% bracket. Fleet discounts up to 15%.
Free estimate response within 2 hours · Free fleet pickup included
Sprinter vans from $3,500 · All lengths and roof heights
Box trucks 16–26 ft · Full cab, sides, and rear coverage
Pickup trucks from $2,200 · F-150, Silverado, Ram
All pricing includes design, print, professional installation, and UV-protective overlaminate. No hidden fees.
Learn more about our fleet wrap program â
Full wraps cover every visible panel for maximum brand impact. They generate the most impressions and deliver the best long-term ROI. Full wraps are the standard for fleet branding programs.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a van wrap cost in Chicago in 2026?**
A: In 2026, cargo van full wraps in Chicago start at $3,150. Sprinter van wraps start at $3,700. Partial van wraps start at $1,200. Pricing depends on vehicle size, coverage, and design complexity.

**Q: How much does a box truck wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Box truck wrap pricing in Chicago: 16-20 ft trucks start at $4,500, 22-26 ft trucks start at $6,700. Full wraps cover the cab, sides, and rear. Free estimates available.

**Q: Are vehicle wraps tax deductible for businesses?**
A: Yes! Vehicle wraps are 100% tax deductible as a business advertising expense under IRS Section 179. This means a $3,150 van wrap effectively costs your business significantly less after the deduction.

**Q: Do vehicle wrap prices include design?**
A: Yes — our vehicle wrap pricing includes in-house custom design, unlimited revisions, print, laminate, and professional installation. Free fleet pickup and delivery is also included throughout Chicagoland.

About Chicago Fleet Wraps — 24+ Years Wrapping Chicago
Chicago Fleet Wraps delivers premium vehicle wrap solutions with 24+ years of experience, 9,400+ successful installations, and zero verified paint damage claims. We service all of Chicago and surrounding areas with professional installation, free estimates, and 2-year workmanship warranty.

With over 24 years of experience and 9,400+ vehicles wrapped, Chicago Fleet Wraps stands as Chicago's most trusted vehicle wrap provider. Our commitment to excellence spans every project, from single-vehicle wraps to comprehensive fleet programs.
Our team has wrapped everything from single-car owners to large commercial fleets. We specialize in:
Vehicle wraps deliver exceptional ROI compared to traditional advertising. Research shows wrapped vehicles generate 4x more views than static billboards at a fraction of the cost.
Quality wraps using premium materials typically last 5-7 years with proper care. UV exposure, climate, and maintenance affect longevity. We use fade-resistant 3M and Avery Dennison materials rated for long-term outdoor exposure.
Yes. Professional removal by experienced installers ensures zero damage to original paint. We've removed thousands of wraps without incident, protecting your vehicle's resale value.

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsService Area — 75+ Cities Across 6 Chicagoland Counties
Chicago Fleet Wraps provides free pickup and delivery for every fleet wrap project across the entire Chicagoland metropolitan area. From our climate-controlled facility at 4711 N Lamon Ave, Chicago, IL 60630, we serve over 75 cities and villages spanning Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry counties — a 50-mile radius covering more than 9 million residents and hundreds of thousands of commercial vehicles.
Our shop sits on the northwest side of Chicago in the Old Irving Park / Jefferson Park corridor, giving us fast expressway access to every neighborhood and suburb. The Kennedy (I-90/94), Edens (I-94), Eisenhower (I-290), Stevenson (I-55), and Dan Ryan (I-90/94) expressways put most of the metro within 30–45 minutes of our door.
We break our service territory into six county zones. Every zone receives the same free pickup, same turnaround times, and same 2-year installation warranty.
Our facility at 4711 N Lamon Ave sits just off I-90 (Kennedy Expressway), making pickups fast and efficient. Here are approximate distances and drive times to major cities in our service area:

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: Do you pick up and deliver fleet vehicles for wrapping?**
A: Yes. Chicago Fleet Wraps offers free pickup and delivery for all fleet vehicles within our 50-mile service radius. Same-day scheduling is available for vehicles within 30 miles of our shop at 4711 N Lamon Ave, Chicago, IL 60630.

**Q: How far will you travel to pick up a vehicle?**
A: Our standard free pickup radius covers 50 miles from our Chicago shop, reaching cities like Aurora, Joliet, Waukegan, and Crystal Lake. For fleets of 5+ vehicles beyond 50 miles, we arrange special logistics at no extra charge.

**Q: Which counties do you serve?**
A: We serve six Illinois counties: Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry. This covers the entire Chicagoland metropolitan area and over 75 individual cities and villages.

**Q: Do you charge extra for pickup and delivery?**
A: No. Pickup and delivery is always free within our 50-mile service area. There is no surcharge, no mileage fee, and no minimum order requirement for the free pickup service.

**Q: Can you wrap vehicles at our location?**
A: All wraps are installed at our climate-controlled facility at 4711 N Lamon Ave, Chicago. This ensures proper temperature (70°F), lighting, and dust-free conditions for the best adhesion and finish quality.

**Q: How do I schedule a fleet pickup?**
A: Call (312) 597-1286 or submit a request through our online estimate form. We coordinate pickup windows that minimize your fleet downtime—early morning and evening pickups are available.

**Q: Do you serve the Chicago suburbs?**
A: Yes. We serve over 75 suburbs across six counties including Naperville, Schaumburg, Aurora, Joliet, Evanston, Arlington Heights, Oak Park, and many more. Every suburb receives the same free pickup and delivery service.

â â â â â 4.9 Â· 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsHVAC Fleet Wraps Chicago
Professional fleet wraps for HVAC companies in Chicago and Chicagoland. Your service vans and trucks are parked in residential driveways 8+ hours a day â make every job site a billboard generating 30,000â70,000 daily impressions. We wrap cargo vans, sprinter vans, pickup trucks, and box trucks using Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl. Free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland. 2-year workmanship warranty. Fleet discounts for 3+ vehicles.
HVAC service vehicles are uniquely positioned for maximum advertising impact. Unlike most commercial vehicles, HVAC vans are parked in residential driveways for hours during installations and service calls. Every neighbor walking their dog, every mail carrier, every parent driving past sees your brand â building familiarity and trust that translates directly into phone calls when their furnace goes out or their AC stops working.
Chicago's extreme seasons drive year-round demand for HVAC services. A wrapped van in a suburban neighborhood during a January cold snap or July heat wave becomes the most trusted name on the block. Homeowners don't search Google when their furnace fails at 2 AM â they call the company they've seen in their neighborhood. At $0.04â$0.07 per thousand impressions, fleet wraps cost a fraction of Google Ads, mailers, or Angi leads â with zero recurring cost.
The ROI math is clear: a single HVAC van wrap costing $3,500 generates an estimated 16+ million impressions over its 5-year lifespan. Compare that to digital advertising at $3.56 CPM â the equivalent ad spend would be $57,000+. Fleet wraps are the single most cost-effective marketing investment available to HVAC companies in the Chicago market.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do HVAC fleet wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: HVAC cargo van wraps start at $2,800â$4,500, sprinter vans at $3,500â$5,200, pickup trucks at $2,200â$3,800, and box trucks at $4,200â$7,500. Fleet discounts of 5â15% apply for 3+ vehicles. All HVAC fleet wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179.

**Q: Why should HVAC companies wrap their vehicles?**
A: HVAC service vans are parked in residential driveways for hours during service calls â generating thousands of neighborhood impressions. A wrapped van builds trust before the technician rings the doorbell and generates referrals from neighbors who see your brand daily. At $0.04 CPM, fleet wraps cost 90x less than Google Ads.

**Q: How long do HVAC fleet wraps last?**
A: Our HVAC fleet wraps use premium Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl rated for 5â7 years of outdoor durability. Combined with a 2-year installation warranty, HVAC companies get years of advertising from a single investment.

**Q: Can you wrap HVAC trucks with equipment racks and ladders?**
A: Yes. We template every vehicle individually and cut the wrap to fit around ladder racks, roof-mounted equipment, condenser dollies, and toolbox modifications. The wrap looks clean and professional regardless of aftermarket accessories.

**Q: What information should an HVAC van wrap include?**
A: The most effective HVAC van wraps include your company name, phone number (large and readable from 50+ feet), services offered (heating, cooling, installation, repair), website URL, and license number. We also recommend including '24/7 Emergency Service' if applicable â it's the #1 driver of calls from van wraps.

**Q: Are HVAC fleet wraps tax deductible?**
A: Yes â HVAC fleet wraps are 100% tax deductible as a business advertising expense under IRS Section 179. Many HVAC companies wrap their fleets in Q4 to maximize their annual tax deduction.

**Q: How quickly can you wrap an HVAC fleet?**
A: Individual vehicles take 2â3 business days. For multi-vehicle HVAC fleets, we stagger installation to keep your fleet running â typically wrapping 1â2 vehicles at a time. A 10-vehicle fleet can be completed in 3â4 weeks with zero downtime.

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsPlumber Fleet Wraps Chicago
Professional fleet wraps for plumbing companies in Chicago and Chicagoland. Your service vans are your best marketing tool — wrapped plumbing vans generate 30,000–70,000 daily impressions while parked at job sites and driving between appointments. We wrap cargo vans, sprinter vans, pickup trucks, and box trucks using Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl. Free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland. 2-year workmanship warranty.
Plumbing emergencies don't wait — and neither does your advertising. A wrapped plumbing van parked in a driveway during a pipe burst, sewer backup, or water heater replacement becomes the most trusted name on the block. Neighbors see your brand, note your phone number, and call you when they need help. It's the most organic, trust-building form of advertising available to plumbing companies in Chicago.
In Chicago's competitive plumbing market, brand recognition is everything. Homeowners searching for a plumber often choose the company they've seen in their neighborhood — not the first Google result. A fleet of wrapped vans creates the perception of a large, established, professional operation — whether you have 3 vans or 30.
The economics are undeniable: at $0.04 CPM (cost per thousand impressions), plumbing fleet wraps deliver 90x more impressions per dollar than Google Ads ($3.56 CPM). A single $3,500 van wrap generates an estimated 16+ million impressions over its 5-year lifespan — the equivalent of $57,000+ in digital ad spend. With zero recurring cost and 100% tax deductibility, fleet wraps are the highest-ROI marketing investment for plumbing companies.
Fleet discounts: 5% for 3–4 vehicles, 10% for 5–9 vehicles, 15% for 10+ vehicles. All plumbing fleet wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179. See full pricing details →
Chicago's aging housing stock — much of it built before 1960 — creates constant demand for plumbing services. From the bungalow belt on the Northwest Side to the vintage two-flats in Lincoln Park, Chicago homes require frequent plumbing repairs, repipes, and sewer line replacements. This means plumbing vans are parked in residential neighborhoods across the city every single day.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do plumbing van wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Plumbing cargo van wraps start at $2,800–$4,500, sprinter vans at $3,500–$5,200, pickup trucks at $2,200–$3,800, and box trucks at $4,200–$7,500. Fleet discounts of 5–15% apply for 3+ vehicles. All wraps are 100% tax deductible.

**Q: Why do plumbing companies wrap their vans?**
A: Plumbing vans parked in driveways during service calls generate thousands of neighborhood impressions. A branded van builds trust and generates referrals from neighbors — at a CPM of $0.04 vs $3.56 for digital ads. Many plumbers report wraps pay for themselves within months.

**Q: Are plumbing fleet wraps tax deductible?**
A: Yes — fleet wraps are 100% tax deductible as a business advertising expense under IRS Section 179. The full cost can be deducted in the year of installation.

**Q: How long does a plumbing van wrap last?**
A: Our wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5–7 years of outdoor durability. Combined with a 2-year installation warranty, plumbing companies get years of daily advertising from a single investment.

**Q: What should a plumbing van wrap include?**
A: Effective plumbing van wraps include the company name, large phone number, services list (drains, water heaters, remodel, emergency), license number, and website. '24/7 Emergency Plumbing' is the highest-converting callout.

**Q: Can you wrap plumbing vans with pipe racks?**
A: Yes. We template every vehicle individually and cut wraps around pipe racks, ladder racks, toolboxes, and other aftermarket equipment for a clean professional finish.

**Q: Do you offer partial wraps for plumbing companies?**
A: Yes. Partial wraps covering sides, rear, and hood start at $1,200–$2,400. They're a cost-effective option for growing plumbing companies that still want professional branding.

â â â â â 4.9 Â· 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsFood Truck Wraps Chicago
Custom food truck wraps and graphics in Chicago. Your food truck is your brand â make it unforgettable with vibrant, full-coverage wraps that draw crowds at festivals, street corners, and corporate events. We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl with FDA-compliant, food-safe overlaminate. Heat-resistant adhesive rated for kitchen-adjacent surfaces. 2-year workmanship warranty. Free consultation.
Chicago's food truck scene is one of the most competitive in the country. From Wacker Drive lunch spots to Navy Pier events, Daley Plaza festivals, and suburban farmers markets â your food truck competes for attention against dozens of other vendors. A professionally wrapped food truck with bold colors, appetizing photography, and clear branding draws customers from 50+ feet away â before they can even smell the food.
Beyond events, your food truck is a moving billboard. Driving to and from events, parked in commissary lots, and featured in every Instagram photo your customers post â your wrap generates tens of thousands of impressions daily. Many food truck operators report that their wrap pays for itself within the first month through increased customer traffic and social media exposure.
Chicago's food truck regulations require clear signage including your business name and license number. A professionally designed wrap ensures you're fully compliant while looking absolutely stunning â the kind of food truck that becomes a local landmark.
Step 1: Consultation & Photography â We photograph your truck from every angle and take precise measurements of every panel, window, vent, and equipment mount. This creates an exact-scale digital template.
Step 2: Design Concepts â Our designers create 2â3 initial concepts incorporating your logo, brand colors, menu highlights, social media handles, and contact information. We show photorealistic mockups from every angle.
Step 3: Revisions & Approval â Unlimited revisions until you're 100% satisfied. We ensure the design works at speed (highway visibility), at rest (event lines), and on social media (Instagram-worthy).
Step 4: Print & Install â HP Latex 800W wide-format printing for vivid, food-safe colors. FDA-compliant overlaminate. Heat-resistant adhesive for kitchen-adjacent surfaces. 3â5 day installation.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a food truck wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Food truck full wraps range from $4,200â$8,000 depending on truck size and design complexity. Partial wraps and accent graphics start at $1,500â$3,000. Menu board graphics start at $400â$1,200.

**Q: Are food truck wraps food-safe?**
A: Yes. All food truck wraps use FDA-compliant, food-safe overlaminate. Heat-resistant adhesive is used for kitchen-adjacent surfaces near grills, fryers, and steam â ensuring the wrap stays intact and doesn't contaminate food contact areas.

**Q: How long does a food truck wrap last?**
A: Our food truck wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5â7 years of outdoor durability. The overlaminate protects against grease splatter, steam, UV exposure, and frequent cleaning.

**Q: Can you wrap around serving windows and equipment?**
A: Yes. We template every food truck individually to account for serving windows, exhaust vents, generators, propane tanks, and equipment. The wrap is precision-cut for a seamless professional finish.

**Q: Do you design food truck wraps?**
A: Yes. Our in-house design team specializes in food truck graphics that sell. We create exact-scale templates, full-color mockups, and incorporate your logo, menu highlights, social media handles, and contact information.

**Q: How long does food truck wrap installation take?**
A: Most food truck wraps take 3â5 business days for design, print, and installation. We can accommodate rush timelines for festival season or grand openings.

**Q: Can you update menu board graphics separately?**
A: Yes. Menu board panels are designed as replaceable sections so you can update your menu without rewrapping the entire truck. Individual panel replacements start at $200â$400.

**Heading:** Vehicle Wrap Removal Chicago — Safe & Paint

## How much does wrap removal cost in Chicago?
## Will removal damage my paint?
## How long does wrap removal take?

## What about old, sun-baked wraps?
## Do you offer fleet wrap removal?
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Wrap Removal Chicago
Professional vehicle wrap removal in Chicago. We safely remove full wraps, partial graphics, fleet lettering, color change wraps, and vinyl decals without damaging your factory paint. Adhesive residue cleanup is always included. Fleet removal pricing available with free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland. Call (312) 597-1286 for a free removal quote.
Whether you're rebranding a fleet, returning a lease, or simply want a fresh start, we handle every type of vehicle wrap removal. Every service includes full adhesive residue cleanup — you'll never leave our shop with sticky panels.
Wrap removal is not just peeling — it's a controlled, methodical process that protects your paint at every stage. Here's exactly how we do it:
Why climate control matters: Chicago winters regularly drop below 20°F. At those temperatures, vinyl becomes rigid and brittle. Attempting removal in an unheated garage or outdoors risks cracking the film into tiny pieces that take 3–4x longer to remove and dramatically increase the chance of paint damage. Our facility stays at 70°F year-round.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does wrap removal cost in Chicago?**
A: Wrap removal costs $500–$1,500 for a car and $800–$3,500 for a box truck. Pricing depends on vehicle size, vinyl age, and vinyl type. Older and calendered vinyl takes longer. Adhesive cleanup is always included.

**Q: Will removing a wrap damage my paint?**
A: Not when done properly. We use controlled infrared heat and slow peeling at the correct angle to remove vinyl without lifting or damaging factory paint. Cast vinyl from Avery Dennison and 3M removes cleanly. Calendered vinyl and wraps over 7 years old require extra care but we handle them regularly.

**Q: How long does wrap removal take?**
A: 1–2 business days for a car or van. 2–3 days for a box truck. Same-day removal is available for partial graphics and decals. Fleet removal is batched for efficiency.

**Q: Can you remove a wrap and re-wrap the same vehicle?**
A: Yes. Removal plus re-wrap is one of our most common services. We remove the old wrap, prep the surface, and install the new wrap in a single visit.

**Q: Do you remove wraps from leased vehicles?**
A: Yes. Lease-return wrap removal is a common service. We ensure the vehicle is returned in original condition with no adhesive residue or damage.

**Q: What about adhesive residue after removal?**
A: Residue removal is included in every wrap removal service. We use controlled heat and specialized citrus-based adhesive removers — no harsh chemicals that could damage clear coat or paint.

**Q: Do you offer fleet wrap removal?**
A: Yes. We offer fleet removal pricing with free pickup and delivery. Common when companies rebrand, update designs, or return leased vehicles.

**Q: Does Chicago weather affect wrap removal?**
A: Yes. Cold temperatures make vinyl more brittle and harder to remove cleanly. We perform all removal work in our heated, climate-controlled facility at 70°F to ensure safe, consistent results regardless of outside weather.


---

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Lettering Chicago
Professional vinyl lettering for commercial vehicles, fleet trucks, and service vans in Chicago. The fastest, most affordable way to brand your vehicles with your company name, phone number, logo, and DOT numbers. Cut vinyl lettering lasts 7–10 years and starts at just $150 for DOT numbers.
Cut vinyl lettering uses solid-color vinyl cut into individual letters and shapes. It's clean, professional, and extremely durable — lasting 7–10 years outdoors. For simple text and single-color logos, cut vinyl is the most cost-effective and longest-lasting option.
Printed graphics allow full-color logos, photos, and complex designs. We print on premium Avery Dennison cast vinyl with UV overlaminate for 5–7 years of outdoor durability. Best for multi-color logos and photographic elements.
The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) requires USDOT numbers on both sides of all commercial vehicles that meet any of these criteria:
Requirements: letters must be at least 2 inches tall, in a color that contrasts with the vehicle's background, and visible from 50 feet. We ensure full FMCSA compliance on every DOT lettering job.
Need consistent lettering across 5, 10, or 50+ vehicles? We offer fleet lettering programs with:
DOT numbers start at $150. Basic lettering (name + phone) starts at $250. Full packages with logo, name, phone, and website start at $500.
USDOT numbers are required on both sides of commercial vehicles over 10,001 lbs GVW. Letters must be 2"+ tall in a contrasting color. We ensure full FMCSA compliance.
Cut vinyl lettering lasts 7–10 years outdoors. Printed graphics last 5–7 years with UV overlaminate.
Cut vinyl for simple text and single-color logos. Printed graphics for full-color logos and complex designs.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does vehicle lettering cost?**
A: Vehicle lettering starts at $250–$800 depending on text amount, colors, and vehicle size. DOT number packages start at $150. Fleet lettering programs with volume pricing available.

**Q: What are DOT number requirements?**
A: FMCSA requires USDOT numbers on both sides of commercial vehicles over 10,001 lbs GVW or hauling hazardous materials. Letters must be at least 2 inches tall in a contrasting color. We ensure full compliance.

**Q: How long does vinyl lettering last?**
A: Cut vinyl lettering lasts 7–10 years outdoors. It's more durable than printed graphics because there's no ink layer to fade — the color is the vinyl itself.

**Q: Cut vinyl vs. printed graphics — which is better?**
A: Cut vinyl is better for simple text and single-color logos — it's cleaner, more durable, and more affordable. Printed graphics are better for full-color logos, photos, and complex designs.

**Q: Can you letter a fleet of vehicles consistently?**
A: Yes. Fleet lettering programs ensure consistent sizing, placement, and colors across 5, 10, or 50+ vehicles. Templates are stored for future additions.

**Q: Do you offer same-day lettering?**
A: Simple DOT numbers and basic lettering can often be produced and installed same-day or next-day. Contact us for rush availability.

## [How Much Does a Car Wrap Cost in Chicago?](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/how-much-does-a-car-wrap-cost/)
> Real vehicle wrap pricing in Chicago. Sedans, SUVs, cargo vans, sprinters, box trucks. Premium cast vinyl. Chicago Fleet Wraps.

## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsHow Much Does a Car Wrap Cost in Chicago?
Vehicle wrap pricing depends on vehicle size, coverage level, and material quality. Here's what to expect in the Chicago market with premium cast vinyl:
These prices include design, print, premium cast vinyl (Avery Dennison MPI 1105 or 3M IJ180-CV3), UV overlaminate, and certified installation. Fleet discounts: 5% off 3–4 vehicles, 10% off 5–9, 15% off 10+.
Beware of shops quoting under $2,000 for a full car wrap — they're almost certainly using calendered vinyl that will fail within 1–2 years.

## [SUV Wraps for Business: Chicago Professionals Guide | CFW](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/suv-wraps-business-branding-chicago-sales-real-estate/)
> Transform your SUV into a mobile billboard! Complete guide to SUV wraps for Chicago sales teams, real estate agents & mobile pros. Free estimate today.
**Heading:** SUV Wraps for Business: Complete Guide for Chicago Professionals

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsSUV Wraps for Business: Complete Guide for Chicago Professionals
SUVs offer maximum visibility with their larger surface area and higher profile on the road. Whether you're in real estate, sales, or any mobile profession in Chicago, an SUV wrap transforms your vehicle into a 24/7 marketing machine.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does it cost to wrap an SUV in Chicago?**
A: SUV wrap costs in Chicago typically range from $2,500 to $4,500 for a full wrap. Partial wraps start around $1,200-$2,000. Fleet discounts available up to 15%.

**Q: How long do SUV wraps last in Chicago weather?**
A: High-quality SUV wraps using 3M or Avery Dennison cast vinyl typically last 5-7 years in Chicago's climate with proper care.

**Q: Will wrapping my SUV damage the original paint?**
A: When properly installed and removed by professionals, high-quality cast vinyl wraps actually protect your SUV's original paint from UV damage, minor scratches, and road debris.

> Transform your Chicago business with professional window graphics & perforated vinyl. 24+ years experience, 2,800+ fleet accounts. Get your free estimate today!
**Heading:** Chicago Window Graphics: Perforated Vinyl for Fleets & Stores

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsChicago Window Graphics: Perforated Vinyl for Fleets & Stores
Window graphics and perforated vinyl offer Chicago businesses a powerful way to maximize advertising space while maintaining visibility and privacy. Learn how these versatile solutions can transform your fleet vehicles and storefronts.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do window graphics cost for commercial vehicles in Chicago?**
A: Window graphics for commercial vehicles typically range from $200-800 per vehicle, depending on size, complexity, and quantity. Fleet discounts start at 3% for 2-4 vehicles.

**Q: Do perforated window graphics affect visibility while driving?**
A: High-quality perforated vinyl reduces interior visibility by approximately 10-20%, similar to wearing lightly tinted sunglasses. Most drivers adapt within minutes.

**Q: How long do window graphics last in Chicago weather?**
A: Professional-grade window graphics installed with premium materials typically last 5-7 years in Chicago's climate with proper maintenance.

## [Top Benefits of Custom Decals for Fleet Vehicles](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/top-benefits-of-custom-decals/)
> Why fleet decals deliver massive ROI. Cost-effective branding, 24/7 advertising, easy updates. Chicago Fleet Wraps.
**Heading:** Top Benefits of Custom Decals for Your Fleet Vehicles

## 1. Cost-Effective Branding
## 2. Professional Image
## 3. 24/7 Advertising
## 4. Easy to Update
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsTop Benefits of Custom Decals for Your Fleet Vehicles
Custom fleet decals are specially designed graphics that can be customized with your company's logo, branding, and contact information. Here's why they're worth the investment:
Decals and partial wraps start at a fraction of a full wrap's cost while still delivering thousands of daily impressions. A logo, phone number, and website on both sides of a van can generate calls from day one.
Branded vehicles signal legitimacy. Homeowners are far more likely to open their door to a service tech arriving in a professionally branded vehicle than an unmarked one.
Your fleet works for you even when parked. A branded van parked at a job site, in a driveway, or in a parking lot is a stationary billboard seen by every passerby.
Changed your phone number? Added a new service? Decals can be removed and replaced without affecting the vehicle's paint. Full wraps require more effort to update, but individual decal panels are quick and affordable to swap.

Yes. Professional removal by experienced installers ensures zero damage to original paint. We've removed thousands of wraps without incident, protecting your vehicle's resale value.
Pricing depends on vehicle size, design complexity, and material selection. Call (312) 597-1286 for a free, detailed quote. Most partial wraps range $1,500-$3,500; full wraps $2,500-$5,500+.
Most installations complete in 2-5 business days depending on complexity. Simple color change wraps may finish in 1-2 days. We schedule around your operational needs to minimize downtime.

â â â â â 4.9 Â· 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Wrap FAQ â 35+ Questions Answered
Everything fleet managers, business owners, and vehicle enthusiasts need to know about vehicle wraps â from cost and materials to timeline, warranty, paint protection, ROI, and Chicago-specific considerations. We have wrapped 9,400+ vehicles across 2,800+ fleet accounts since 2001.
Pricing depends on vehicle size and coverage. Car wraps (sedans, coupes) start at $2,500â$3,500. Cargo van full wraps start at $3,150. Sprinter vans from $3,700. Box trucks 16â20 ft from $4,500. Box trucks 22â26 ft from $6,700. Bus wraps range $6,000â$15,000+. All pricing includes custom design, premium cast vinyl, professional installation, and a 2-year warranty. Use our Wrap Calculator for instant estimates.
Yes. Partial wraps typically cost 40â60% of a full wrap. A cargo van partial wrap (sides and rear) starts around $1,800â$2,400. Partial wraps are a smart option for businesses with tight budgets that still want high visibility on the road.
Yes. We offer tiered fleet pricing: 5% off for 3â4 vehicles, 10% off for 5â9 vehicles, and 15% off for 10+ vehicles. For large fleet programs (30+ vehicles), we create custom pricing packages. Contact us for a fleet quote.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does it cost to wrap a vehicle?**
A: Vehicle wrap pricing depends on size and coverage. Car wraps start at $2,500â$3,500. Cargo van full wraps start at $3,150. Sprinter vans from $3,700. Box trucks 16â20 ft from $4,500. Box trucks 22â26 ft from $6,700. Bus wraps range $6,000â$15,000+. All pricing includes custom design, premium cast vinyl, professional installation, and a 2-year warranty.

**Q: How long does a vehicle wrap last?**
A: Premium cast vinyl (Avery Dennison MPI 1105 / 3M IJ180-CV3) is rated 5â7 years outdoors. In Chicago's freeze-thaw climate, properly installed wraps regularly achieve 6â7 years. Chicago Fleet Wraps backs every installation with a 2-year workmanship warranty.

**Q: Are vehicle wraps tax deductible?**
A: Yes. Commercial vehicle wraps qualify as advertising expenses under IRS Section 179 â 100% deductible in the year of purchase. A $3,150 cargo van wrap in the 28% bracket has an after-tax cost of approximately $2,268. Consult your CPA for specifics.

**Q: Will a wrap damage my vehicle's paint?**
A: No. Premium cast vinyl applied and removed correctly does not damage factory paint. Wraps actually protect the original paint from UV damage, minor scratches, and road debris. Chicago Fleet Wraps has zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years of operations.

**Q: How long does wrap installation take?**
A: Sedans and SUVs: 1 day. Cargo vans: 1â2 business days. Sprinter vans: 2â3 days. Box trucks 16â22 ft: 2â3 days. Box trucks 24â26 ft: 3â4 days. Semi trailers: 4â6 days. Fleet orders are staggered to minimize downtime.

**Q: What vinyl material do you use?**
A: We use only Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 premium cast vinyl â never calendered. Cast vinyl is dimensionally stable, rated 5â7 years outdoor, and handles Chicago's temperature range (-20Â°F to 100Â°F) without shrinking.

**Q: Do you offer free pickup and delivery?**
A: Yes. Free fleet pickup and delivery anywhere within our 50-mile service radius covering Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry counties â over 75 cities.

**Q: What is the difference between cast and calendered vinyl?**
A: Cast vinyl is poured into a mold and cured, making it thinner, more conformable, and longer lasting (5â7 years). Calendered vinyl is pressed through rollers, making it thicker, less flexible, and shorter lived (2â3 years). Chicago Fleet Wraps uses only cast vinyl for all projects.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last?**
A: Quality vehicle wraps typically last 5-7 years with proper care and maintenance. The durability depends on weather conditions, UV exposure, and how well the wrap is maintained.

**Q: Can vehicle wraps be removed without damage?**
A: Yes, professional vehicle wraps can be safely removed without damaging the original paint when installed and removed by experienced professionals like Chicago Fleet Wraps.

**Q: How much does a vehicle wrap cost?**
A: Vehicle wrap costs vary based on size and complexity. Contact Chicago Fleet Wraps at 312-597-1286 for a free quote on your specific project.

**Q: How long does installation take?**
A: Most vehicle wraps take 2-5 business days to complete depending on the complexity and size of the project.

**Q: Do you offer fleet wraps?**
A: Yes, Chicago Fleet Wraps specializes in fleet graphics and commercial wraps for businesses throughout Chicago and surrounding areas.

â â â â â 4.9 · 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsFood Truck Wraps in Chicago
Chicago Fleet Wraps creates stunning food truck wraps for restaurants, caterers, and mobile food vendors across Chicago. Your food truck is your storefront — make it impossible to ignore at festivals, street corners, and events. We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl with FDA-compliant, food-safe overlaminate and heat-resistant adhesive rated for kitchen-adjacent surfaces. 2-year workmanship warranty included.
Chicago's food truck scene is one of the most competitive in the country. From Wacker Drive lunch spots to Navy Pier events and suburban festivals, your food truck competes against dozens of other vendors for attention. A professionally wrapped food truck with bold colors, appetizing photography, and clear branding draws customers from 50+ feet away — before they can even smell the food.
Beyond events, your food truck is a moving billboard. Driving to and from events, parked in commissary lots, and posted on social media — your wrap generates tens of thousands of impressions daily. Many food truck operators report their wrap pays for itself within the first month of increased sales.
All food truck wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179 as a business advertising expense.
Our in-house design team specializes in food truck graphics that sell. We photograph your truck, create exact-scale templates, and produce full-color mockups showing how your wrap will look from every angle. We incorporate your logo, menu highlights, social media handles, and contact information into a cohesive design that works at speed and at rest. Learn about our design process â
Food truck full wraps range from $4,200–$8,000 depending on truck size, design complexity, and number of serving windows. Partial wraps start at $1,500. Menu board graphics start at $400.
Yes. All food truck wraps use FDA-compliant, food-safe overlaminate. We use heat-resistant adhesive for surfaces near grills, fryers, and steam — ensuring the wrap stays in place and doesn't contaminate food contact areas.
Our food truck wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5–7 years of outdoor durability. The overlaminate protects against grease splatter, steam, and UV exposure. Regular cleaning extends wrap life significantly.
Yes. We template every food truck individually to account for serving windows, exhaust vents, generators, and equipment. The wrap is precision-cut to fit around every component for a seamless, professional finish.
- Menu Board Graphics — Printed menu panels for serving windows. $400–$1,200.
- Serving Window Wraps — Counter and awning graphics that complete the look.
- Partial Wraps & Accents — Cost-effective branding for tight budgets. $1,500–$3,000.
- Food Trailer Wraps — Enclosed trailers for catering and event operations.
- Catering Van Wraps — Cargo and sprinter van wraps for catering delivery fleets.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a food truck wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Food truck wraps in Chicago start at $3,500 depending on vehicle size and coverage. Full wraps include all sides, rear, and serving windows. Partial wraps and side-only wraps are also available from $1,800.

**Q: Can a food truck wrap be removed?**
A: Yes! Our Avery Dennison and 3M cast vinyl wraps can be cleanly removed when it's time to rebrand or sell. We've done hundreds of removals with zero paint damage. This flexibility makes wraps ideal for food trucks.

**Q: How long do food truck wraps last?**
A: Food truck wraps using premium cast vinyl last 5-7 years. They're designed to withstand Chicago winters, summer heat, and regular washing. All wraps include a 2-year workmanship warranty.

**Q: Do you design food truck wraps in Chicago?**
A: Yes! Our in-house design team creates custom food truck wrap designs. We work with your brand guidelines, colors, and photography to create a design that attracts customers. Unlimited revisions included.

Ã¢ Ã¢ Ã¢ Ã¢ Ã¢ 4.9 · 41Ã° (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsHVAC Van Wraps in Chicago
Chicago Fleet Wraps specializes in HVAC van wraps for heating and cooling companies across Chicago and Chicagoland. Your service vans spend hours parked in residential driveways during installations and repairs — every job site becomes a neighborhood billboard generating thousands of impressions. We wrap cargo vans, sprinter vans, pickup trucks, and box trucks with premium Avery Dennison and 3M cast vinyl rated for Chicago's extreme seasons.
HVAC service vans are uniquely positioned for maximum advertising impact. Unlike delivery vehicles that stop briefly, HVAC vans park in residential driveways for 2–8 hours during installations and service calls. Every neighbor walking their dog, every parent on the school run, every mail carrier sees your brand — and remembers it when their furnace breaks down in January or their AC fails in July.
Chicago's extreme seasons drive year-round HVAC demand. A professionally wrapped van generates 30,000–70,000 daily impressions at just $0.04 CPM — compared to $3.50+ CPM for Google Ads and $8–$25 per lead on Angi or HomeAdvisor. One wrapped van pays for itself with a single job referral.
All HVAC van wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179 as a business advertising expense.
The most effective HVAC van wraps follow a clear hierarchy: company name visible from 100+ feet, phone number readable from 50 feet, and services listed for close-range reading. We design wraps that work from every angle — crucial because HVAC vans are often viewed from the side while parked in driveways.
We recommend including your Google review rating, key service areas, and a clear call to action. Emergency HVAC companies should prominently feature "24/7" messaging and their phone number in the largest possible font. Learn about our fleet branding process Ã¢
Cargo van wraps start at $2,800–$4,500 for a full wrap. Sprinter vans run $3,500–$5,200. Partial wraps and lettering start at $800–$1,500. Fleet discounts of 5–15% apply for 3+ vehicles.
We wrap all HVAC service vans: Ford Transit, Ram ProMaster, Chevy Express, Mercedes Sprinter, NV200, and Transit Connect. We also wrap pickup trucks and box trucks used for HVAC installations.
Our HVAC van wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5–7 years of outdoor durability in Chicago's freeze-thaw climate. Includes a 2-year installation warranty covering lifting, peeling, and bubbling.
Absolutely. We do precise Pantone and custom color matching to ensure your van wraps match your existing branding, business cards, uniforms, and website.
- Ram ProMaster — 1500, 2500, 3500 cargo and cutaway models.
- Chevy Express — 2500 and 3500 cargo vans for service techs.
- Mercedes Sprinter — Standard and high-roof. Ideal for HVAC companies with large equipment.
- Nissan NV200 / NV2500 — Compact and full-size for residential service calls.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do HVAC van wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: HVAC van wraps in Chicago start at $3,150 for a cargo van full wrap. Sprinter van wraps start at $3,700. HVAC fleet discounts available for 3+ vehicles. All wraps are 100% IRS Section 179 deductible.

**Q: What information should I include on my HVAC van wrap?**
A: Effective HVAC van wraps should include: company name prominently, phone number in large type, license numbers (HVAC license, EPA certification), service area, website, and a clear call-to-action. We handle all design.

**Q: How do HVAC van wraps generate leads in Chicago?**
A: A wrapped HVAC van generates 30,000-70,000 daily impressions in Chicago. OAAA studies show vehicle wraps have a 97% ad recall rate. For HVAC companies, wrapped vans on local routes build brand recognition that drives calls.

**Q: Do you wrap HVAC fleets in Chicago suburbs?**
A: Yes! We serve all HVAC contractors throughout Chicagoland — 75+ cities including Schaumburg, Naperville, Aurora, Joliet, Arlington Heights, and all suburbs. Free fleet pickup included.

We offer free fleet pickup and delivery throughout Joliet with Avery Dennison and 3M premium cast vinyl and a 2-year workmanship warranty.
Joliet is the fastest-growing city in Will County and one of the largest logistics corridors in the Midwest. The I-80 and I-55 interchange makes Joliet a gateway for freight, distribution, and transportation companies â many of which operate fleets of box trucks, cargo vans, and sprinter vans. For these businesses, fleet wraps provide dual value: professional branding for client-facing operations and mobile advertising that works every mile the vehicle travels.
Beyond logistics, Joliet's residential growth has been explosive. New subdivisions in the western and southern parts of the city are driving demand for home construction, HVAC installation, plumbing, and landscaping services. Service companies that wrap their vehicles early in a developing market build brand recognition before competitors even arrive â creating a first-mover advantage that compounds over years of daily exposure.
Joliet's intermodal facilities and warehouse districts make it one of the largest logistics hubs in the Midwest. Box truck and cargo van wraps are essential for distribution companies, freight brokers, and last-mile delivery services operating out of Joliet's industrial parks along I-80 and Larkin Avenue.
The building boom across Will County has created massive demand for contractors, roofers, siding companies, and specialty trades. Wrapped pickup trucks and box trucks parked on active construction sites in new Joliet subdivisions serve as neighborhood billboards â generating leads from homeowners who see the branding every day.
Joliet's growing residential base sustains strong demand for HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services. Service companies wrapping their fleets gain a competitive edge in a market where new homeowners are actively searching for reliable local providers.
Yes â we provide free fleet pickup and delivery anywhere in Joliet and Will County. Whether your vehicles are at a warehouse on I-80 or a job site in the Wesmere subdivision, we'll come to you.
We wrap box trucks from 16 ft to 26 ft with full coverage including cab, sides, and rear. Pricing ranges from $5,000 for a 16 ft truck to $10,900 for a 26 ft truck â common sizes for Joliet-area distribution companies.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do fleet wraps cost in Joliet?**
A: Fleet wrap pricing in Joliet starts at $3,150 for a cargo van full wrap and $4,700 for a box truck. Volume discounts of up to 15% are available for fleets of 10+ vehicles. Contact us for a free estimate at (312) 597-1286.

**Q: Do you offer free vehicle pickup in Joliet?**
A: Yes! Chicago Fleet Wraps offers free fleet pickup and delivery throughout Joliet and all of Chicagoland â 75+ cities. We come to your location, wrap your vehicles, and return them. No downtime hassle.

**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last in Joliet?**
A: We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl, both rated 5-7 years for outdoor durability. Our wraps come with a 2-year workmanship warranty and we have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years.

**Q: What types of vehicles do you wrap?**
A: We wrap cargo vans, sprinter vans, box trucks (16-26 ft), pickup trucks, transit vans, SUVs, electric vehicles (EVs), trailers, and full commercial fleets. We serve trade contractors, delivery companies, and national brands.

Landscaping companies with wrapped fleets consistently report higher close rates on estimates. When a potential customer sees the same branded truck in their neighborhood week after week, trust is already established before the first phone call. Homeowners consistently choose branded landscapers for premium projects like hardscaping, outdoor living spaces, and full landscape renovations.

Chicago's distinct four-season climate creates unique opportunities for landscaping fleet wraps. The spring rush (MarchâMay) is when most new customers choose their landscaper. Companies with wrapped fleets that have been visible in neighborhoods all winter â even if just driving to snow removal jobs â have a massive head start on brand recognition when spring arrives.
The peak season (MayâOctober) is when your wrapped trucks and trailers are parked in neighborhoods for hours every day. A single wrapped truck working a suburban route services 8â15 properties daily, generating thousands of curbside impressions. Multiply that across your fleet and the numbers are staggering.
Smart landscaping companies wrap during the off-season (NovemberâFebruary) when trucks are less busy. This ensures fresh, vibrant branding for the crucial spring selling season. Key suburban markets include Naperville, Wheaton, Downers Grove, Elmhurst, Arlington Heights, and Palatine.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do landscaping truck wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Pickup truck wraps start at $2,200â$3,800, cargo vans at $2,800â$4,500, enclosed trailers at $1,500â$3,500, and box trucks at $4,200â$7,500. Fleet discounts of 5â15% apply for 3+ vehicles. All wraps are 100% tax deductible.

**Q: Can you wrap landscaping trailers?**
A: Yes. We wrap enclosed trailers, open trailers with side panels, dump trailers, and equipment trailers. Trailer wraps start at $1,500 and are the most cost-effective advertising tool for landscapers.

**Q: Do wraps hold up to outdoor conditions?**
A: Absolutely. Our wraps use premium cast vinyl rated for 5â7 years of outdoor durability. They withstand rain, snow, UV exposure, dirt, mud, and regular pressure washing â perfect for landscaping vehicles.

**Q: Are landscaping fleet wraps tax deductible?**
A: Yes â 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179. Many landscaping companies wrap their fleets in the off-season (NovemberâFebruary) to maximize tax benefits and be ready for spring.

**Q: When is the best time to wrap landscaping trucks?**
A: The off-season (NovemberâFebruary) is ideal. Your trucks aren't as busy, installation can happen without disrupting operations, and you'll be ready with fresh branding for the spring rush.

**Q: What should a landscaping truck wrap include?**
A: Effective landscaping wraps include your company name, large phone number, services offered (mowing, landscaping, hardscaping, tree service, snow removal), website URL, and professional photography of your best work.

**Q: Do you wrap snow removal equipment?**
A: Yes. Many landscaping companies offer snow removal in winter. We design wraps that promote both services â lawn care and snow removal â ensuring year-round brand visibility and lead generation.

## [Wrap Materials Guide — Avery Dennison & 3M](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/materials/)
> Compare Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl. Cast vs calendered vinyl specs, durability, and Chicago weather performance. Material guide —.
**Heading:** Vehicle Wrap Materials Guide — Avery & Dennison

## Cast Vinyl vs. Calendered Vinyl
## Side-by-Side Comparison
## UV Overlaminate — Standard on Every Install
## Color Change Films
## Print Technology — HP Latex
## Why Material Matters in Chicago
## Frequently Asked Questions
## What is cast vinyl?
## Cast vs. calendered — what's the difference?
## Avery Dennison or 3M — which is better?
## What overlaminate do you use?
## Do you ever use calendered vinyl?
## Our Location
## Related Pages
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Wrap Materials Guide
Cast vinyl only. No shortcuts. Every vehicle wrap at Chicago Fleet Wraps uses Avery Dennison MPI 1105 or 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl — rated 5–7 years outdoor. We never use calendered vinyl. This guide explains why material choice matters, how cast vinyl outperforms calendered, and what each material specification means for your wrap's longevity.
The single most important factor in a vehicle wrap's longevity is the vinyl type. Cast vinyl is manufactured by pouring liquid PVC onto a casting sheet, producing a thin, flexible film. Calendered vinyl is made by pressing PVC through rollers, creating a thicker, stiffer film with built-in memory stress.
In Chicago's extreme weather — with temperatures ranging from -20°F in January to 100°F in July, plus road salt, UV exposure, and freeze-thaw cycles — calendered vinyl fails predictably within 2–3 years. Cast vinyl handles these conditions for 5–7 years.
Every printed wrap receives a UV-protective overlaminate layer. This clear film protects the printed graphics from UV fading, scratches, chemicals, and road debris.
Color change wraps use pre-colored vinyl film (no printing needed):
All printed vehicle wraps are produced on HP Latex wide-format printers using water-based latex inks. HP Latex offers several advantages over solvent and UV printers:
Chicago's climate is one of the harshest environments for vehicle wraps in the United States. The combination of extreme cold, road salt (October through April), intense summer UV, and daily temperature swings puts tremendous stress on vinyl films. Cast vinyl is specifically engineered to handle these conditions. Calendered vinyl is not.
When choosing a wrap shop, always ask what material they use. If the answer isn't cast vinyl from Avery Dennison or 3M, your wrap will fail prematurely.
Cast vinyl is made by pouring liquid PVC onto a casting sheet. This produces thin, flexible film that conforms to curves and rivets. Rated 5–7 years outdoor.
Cast vinyl is thin, flexible, and lasts 5–7 years. Calendered is thicker, stiffer, cheaper — but shrinks, lifts, and fails in 2–3 years. We only use cast.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What is cast vinyl?**
A: Cast vinyl is manufactured by pouring liquid PVC onto a casting sheet. This produces a thin (2–3 mil), flexible film that conforms to curves and rivets without lifting. It's rated 5–7 years outdoor and is the industry standard for vehicle wraps.

**Q: What is the difference between cast and calendered vinyl?**
A: Cast vinyl is thin, flexible, and durable (5–7 years). Calendered vinyl is thicker, stiffer, and cheaper — but it shrinks, lifts at edges, and fades within 2–3 years. Cast vinyl is the only acceptable material for vehicle wraps.

**Q: Which is better: Avery Dennison or 3M?**
A: Both are excellent. Avery Dennison MPI 1105 has EZ Apply RS adhesive that's slightly easier to work with. 3M IJ180-CV3 has Controltac Comply adhesive that allows repositioning. Both are rated 7 years outdoor. We use both depending on application.

**Q: What overlaminate do you use?**
A: Avery Dennison DOL 1000Z (gloss) or DOL 1300Z (matte). 3M 8518 (gloss) or 8520 (matte). Overlaminate is applied over every printed wrap to protect against UV, scratches, and chemicals.

**Q: Do you use calendered vinyl?**
A: Never. We exclusively use cast vinyl. Calendered vinyl shrinks, lifts, and fails in 2–3 years. It's not suitable for vehicle wraps, especially in Chicago's extreme weather.

Chicago Fleet Wraps designs and installs professional moving truck wraps for moving companies across Chicago and Chicagoland. Moving trucks are the largest mobile billboards on the road — a single wrapped 26-ft box truck generates 50,000–70,000 daily impressions driving through residential neighborhoods during peak moving season. We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl with a 2-year workmanship warranty. Free fleet pickup throughout Chicagoland.
Moving trucks spend all day in residential neighborhoods — the exact places where future customers live. A professionally wrapped moving truck parked in a driveway for 4–8 hours during a move generates hundreds of close-range impressions from neighbors, dog walkers, and passing cars. Chicago has over 100,000 residential moves per year, and a branded truck builds instant recognition and trust.
Unlike digital ads that disappear when you stop paying, a wrapped moving truck works 24/7 — on the highway, at storage facilities, and parked at your yard. At $0.04 CPM, vehicle wraps deliver the lowest cost-per-impression of any advertising medium. Many moving companies report that a single wrapped truck generates 3–5 direct leads per week.
All moving truck wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179 as a business advertising expense.
The most effective moving truck wraps feature company name visible from 200+ feet (box truck sides are massive canvases), a phone number in the largest font possible, your website and Google review rating, and 2–3 key service areas. Include your license/DOT numbers for credibility. Bold, clean designs with high contrast outperform cluttered layouts — your truck is moving at speed and needs to communicate in seconds. Learn about our fleet branding process â

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do moving truck wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Moving box truck wraps start at $4,700 for 16-20 ft trucks and $6,700 for 22-26 ft trucks. Cargo van wraps start at $3,150. Moving company fleet discounts available for 3+ vehicles.

**Q: What moving vehicles do you wrap in Chicago?**
A: We wrap all moving company vehicles: 12 ft to 26 ft box trucks, cargo vans, pickup trucks with flatbeds, and enclosed trailers. We handle single vehicles and full moving fleet programs.

**Q: Do moving truck wraps generate more bookings?**
A: Yes! A wrapped moving truck serves as a rolling advertisement 24/7. When people see your branded truck in their neighborhood, they're more likely to call you when they need to move. 30,000+ daily impressions per truck.

**Q: How do I get a moving truck wrap in Chicago?**
A: Call (312) 597-1286 or request an estimate online. We respond within 2 hours. Free fleet pickup throughout Chicagoland. We can wrap multiple moving trucks simultaneously to minimize downtime.

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsMoving Company Truck Wraps Chicago
Professional fleet wraps for moving companies in Chicago and Chicagoland. Your moving trucks are the largest mobile billboards on the road — a wrapped 26-ft box truck on the Kennedy Expressway generates 70,000+ daily impressions. We wrap box trucks, cargo vans, pickup trucks, and entire moving fleets using Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl. Free pickup and delivery. 2-year workmanship warranty.
Moving trucks are the largest vehicles on the road — and the most visible. A wrapped 26-ft box truck has over 200 square feet of advertising space on each side, making it a rolling billboard that dominates attention on expressways, residential streets, and apartment building parking lots. Customers naturally trust branded movers over unmarked trucks, leading to higher booking rates and the ability to charge premium pricing.
Chicago's competitive moving market rewards companies that invest in brand presence. When your wrapped truck is parked at an apartment building on moving day, every resident sees your brand — the doorman, the neighbors in the lobby, the people walking on the sidewalk. Your trucks work double duty: delivering moving services and generating new leads simultaneously.
The trust factor is enormous. Moving is one of the most stressful life events, and customers are entrusting strangers with their most valuable possessions. A branded, professional-looking fleet immediately communicates legitimacy, insurance, and accountability — the exact qualities customers look for when choosing a mover. Unmarked trucks, by contrast, raise red flags and drive customers to competitors.
Fleet discounts: 5% for 3–4 vehicles, 10% for 5–9 vehicles, 15% for 10+ vehicles. All moving company wraps are 100% tax deductible under IRS Section 179. See full pricing →
Chicago is one of the busiest moving markets in the country. With 2.7 million residents in the city proper and 9.5 million in the metro area, thousands of moves happen every day. The peak moving season (May–September) sees intense competition, with hundreds of moving companies vying for the same customers.
Chicago's unique housing landscape — from downtown high-rises and Lincoln Park walk-ups to suburban single-family homes in Naperville, Schaumburg, and Arlington Heights — means moving trucks are visible across every type of neighborhood. A wrapped truck loading furniture on a busy Lakeview street is seen by hundreds of passersby in a single afternoon.
The trust factor is especially critical in Chicago's moving market, where scam movers have made headlines. A professionally wrapped fleet with your company name, phone number, USDOT number, and website immediately separates you from fly-by-night operators. Customers actively choose branded movers because the wrap signals insurance, accountability, and professionalism.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a moving truck wrap cost in Chicago?**
A: Box truck wraps range from $4,200–$10,000 depending on size (16–26 ft). Cargo van wraps start at $2,800. Pickup trucks start at $2,200. Fleet discounts of 5–15% apply for 3+ vehicles. All wraps are 100% tax deductible.

**Q: Are moving truck wraps worth the investment?**
A: A wrapped 26-ft moving truck on Chicago expressways generates 70,000+ daily impressions. Customers consistently choose branded movers over unmarked trucks, leading to higher booking rates and premium pricing. At $0.04 CPM, wraps deliver 90x more value per dollar than Google Ads.

**Q: Can you wrap leased moving trucks?**
A: Yes. Vinyl wraps are non-permanent and can be professionally removed without damaging paint. Many moving companies wrap leased box trucks and return them in perfect condition at lease end.

**Q: How long does installation take for a moving fleet?**
A: Individual box trucks take 2–4 business days. For multi-truck fleets, we stagger installation to keep your fleet operational — typically wrapping 1–2 trucks at a time.

**Q: What should a moving truck wrap include?**
A: Effective moving truck wraps include the company name, large phone number, website URL, services (local, long distance, packing, storage), USDOT number (required for interstate movers), and customer review score.

**Q: Do you wrap junk removal trucks too?**
A: Yes. Junk removal, hauling, and cleanout companies benefit from the same billboard-style box truck wraps. The same principles apply — large phone number, clear services, professional branding.

**Q: Can you match branding across different truck sizes?**
A: Yes. Our fleet branding program creates consistent designs that scale across 16-ft, 20-ft, and 26-ft box trucks, cargo vans, and pickup trucks — so your entire fleet looks cohesive regardless of vehicle size.
