## What Partial Wraps Include
## When Partial Wraps Make Sense
## Cost Comparison: Partial vs. Full Wraps
## Frequently Asked Questions
## How much do partial vehicle wraps cost?
## What does a partial wrap include?
## When should I choose a partial wrap over a full wrap?
â â â â â 4.9 · 41ð (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsPartial Vehicle Wraps in Chicago
Partial vehicle wraps in Chicago deliver 60–80% of the visual impact of a full wrap at 40–60% of the cost. Strategic coverage on your vehicle's highest-visibility panels — sides, rear doors, and hood — creates a professional, branded appearance without wrapping every surface. Starting at $1,200. Avery Dennison & 3M cast vinyl. 2-year workmanship warranty.
Partial wraps are custom-designed to maximize your brand visibility on the most-seen panels of your vehicle. Common partial wrap configurations include:
Partial wraps start at $1,200 for pickup trucks and go up to $2,800 for sprinter vans. That's 40–60% of full wrap cost while delivering 60–80% of the visual impact.
Partial wraps typically cover the sides, rear doors, and sometimes the hood — the highest-visibility panels. The exact coverage is customized to your vehicle, brand, and budget.
Choose a partial wrap when starting fleet branding on a budget, when your vehicle color matches your brand, when wrapping leased vehicles, or when you want impactful branding without full-coverage cost.
- Rear Doors / Tailgate — The panel most seen by following traffic at stoplights and in traffic.
- Hood Wrap — Eye-catching hood graphics visible to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.
- Lower Body Accent — Rocker panel and lower door graphics that tie the design together.
- Cab-Only (Box Trucks) — Wrap just the cab of a box truck for budget-conscious fleets.
- Budget-Conscious Branding — Get professional branding at 40–60% of full wrap cost.
- Vehicle Color Match — If your vehicle color matches your brand, partial wraps add graphics without covering the matching paint.
- Starting a Fleet — Partial wraps let you brand your first 3–5 vehicles affordably, then upgrade to full wraps later.
- Leased Vehicles — Partial wraps reduce cost and install time on leased fleet vehicles.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do partial vehicle wraps cost in Chicago?**
A: Partial vehicle wraps in Chicago start at $1,200 for basic door or tailgate graphics. Half wraps typically range from $1,800-$2,400. Full wrap pricing starts at $3,150. Free estimates available — call (312) 597-1286.

**Q: What is the difference between a partial and full vehicle wrap?**
A: A full wrap covers the entire vehicle surface. A partial wrap covers specific sections — doors, hood, tailgate, or a defined portion. Partial wraps cost less while still providing strong brand visibility.

**Q: Can a partial wrap be removed without damaging the paint?**
A: Yes! Our Avery Dennison and 3M cast vinyl wraps can be removed cleanly without damaging factory paint when properly installed. We have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years.

**Q: Do spot graphics and decals count as vehicle wraps?**
A: Spot graphics (cut vinyl decals, logo placements) are different from wraps but we offer both. Spot graphics start from $150. Vehicle lettering and logo installs are also available as budget-friendly branding options.

A full wrap covers 100% of the vehicle and delivers maximum brand impact. A partial wrap covers 40–70% of the vehicle — focusing on the areas most visible in traffic, at job sites, and in parking lots. Studies show partial wraps deliver 70–80% of the visual impact of a full wrap at significantly less cost.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a partial wrap cost?**
A: Partial wraps start at $800–$1,500 for basic door panels and go up to $2,500–$3,500 for half wraps with multiple panels. Spot graphics and decals start at $400.

**Q: What is the difference between a full wrap and a partial wrap?**
A: A full wrap covers 100% of the vehicle's exterior. A partial wrap covers 40–70% of the vehicle — typically the highest-visibility areas like sides, tailgate, and hood.

**Q: Is a partial wrap worth it?**
A: Yes. Partial wraps deliver 70–80% of the visual impact of a full wrap at 40–60% of the cost. They're ideal for budget-conscious fleet branding.

**Q: What areas do partial wraps cover?**
A: Common partial wrap configurations include door panels, rear half, upper half, tailgate, hood, and accent graphics. We recommend covering the highest-visibility areas for your vehicle type.

**Q: Do partial wraps use the same materials as full wraps?**
A: Yes. Every partial wrap uses the same Avery Dennison MPI 1105 or 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl as our full wraps. No shortcuts on materials.

**Q: Can I upgrade from a partial wrap to a full wrap later?**
A: Yes. We store your design files so adding panels to expand a partial wrap into a full wrap is straightforward. The additional panels match the original install.

## [5 Tips for Designing Effective Vehicle Branding](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/5-tips-for-designing-effective-vehicle-branding-for-your-fleet/)
> 5 expert tips for designing effective vehicle branding for your fleet. Logo placement, color strategy, readability, and more.
**Heading:** 5 Tips for Designing Effective Vehicle Branding for Your Fleet

## 1. Keep It Simple
## 2. Prioritize Readability
## 3. Design for the Vehicle Shape
## 4. Include a Clear Call-to-Action
## 5. Maintain Brand Consistency
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet Wraps5 Tips for Designing Effective Vehicle Branding for Your Fleet
A great fleet wrap starts with great design. Here are five tips from 24+ years of wrapping Chicago fleets.
You have 3–5 seconds to make an impression on a passing driver. Focus on your logo, phone number, and one key service — not a paragraph of text.
Use high-contrast colors and large, bold fonts. If your wrap can't be read from 50 feet away, it's not working. Sans-serif fonts outperform decorative scripts every time.
Every vehicle has curves, handles, and windows that affect the design. Work with an experienced designer who creates templates specific to your exact make and model.
Your phone number should be the largest text on the vehicle. Add your website and a simple CTA like "Call for Free Estimate" to drive responses.
Use the same colors, fonts, and logo placement across every vehicle in your fleet. Consistency builds recognition — and recognition builds trust.

## [Best Vinyl for Commercial Vehicle Wraps](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/best-vinyl-for-commercial-vehicle-wraps/)
> Compare the best vinyl films for commercial vehicle wraps: Avery Dennison MPI 1105, 3M IJ180-CV3, and more. Durability ratings, costs, and recommendations.

## Cast vs. Calendered: The Critical Difference
## Top Commercial Wrap Films Compared
## Our Top Pick: Avery Dennison MPI 1105
## Runner-Up: 3M IJ180-CV3
## What About Cheap Vinyl?
## Frequently Asked Questions
## What is the best vinyl for commercial vehicle wraps?
## What's the difference between cast and calendered vinyl?
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsBest Vinyl for Commercial Vehicle Wraps
Not all vehicle wrap vinyl is created equal. The film you choose determines how long your wrap lasts, how it looks after 3 years in Chicago weather, and whether it comes off cleanly when it's time to rebrand. Here's our expert comparison of the best commercial-grade vinyl films based on 24+ years and 9,400+ vehicle wraps.
Cast vinyl (2 mil thickness) is manufactured by pouring liquid PVC onto a casting sheet, creating a thin, flexible film that conforms to curves, rivets, and compound shapes without shrinking. Cast vinyl lasts 5–7+ years outdoors.
Calendered vinyl (3–4 mil) is made by squeezing PVC through rollers, creating a thicker, stiffer film with "memory" — it wants to return to its flat state. Calendered vinyl lasts 3–5 years and is prone to shrinkage, cracking, and edge lifting on curved vehicle surfaces.
Our recommendation: Always use cast vinyl for vehicle wraps. The price difference is minimal ($200–$500 more per vehicle), but the performance difference is dramatic. We never use calendered vinyl on vehicle wraps. Learn about our materials →
For commercial fleet wraps, Avery Dennison MPI 1105 with DOL 1460 overlaminate is our primary recommendation. This film offers the best combination of print quality, conformability, clean removability, and outdoor durability (7+ years). It's what we use on 70%+ of our fleet projects.
3M's Controltac cast vinyl with Comply adhesive is an excellent alternative. The pressure-activated adhesive allows repositioning during installation, and the Comply channels allow air to escape for a bubble-free finish. Paired with 3M 8518 overlaminate, it delivers 5–7 years of outdoor performance.
Beware of shops offering vehicle wraps at $1,500–$2,000 for a full van wrap. They're almost certainly using cheap calendered vinyl or low-grade cast films. These wraps look fine for 6–12 months, then begin to shrink, crack, fade, and lift — making your business look worse than an unwrapped vehicle. You'll end up paying for removal and a re-wrap, spending more than if you'd used quality materials from the start.
Avery Dennison MPI 1105 with DOL 1460 overlaminate — 7+ year outdoor rating, excellent conformability, and clean removability. 3M IJ180-CV3 with 8518 overlaminate is an excellent alternative at 5–7 years.
Cast vinyl is thinner, more conformable, and lasts 5–7+ years. Calendered vinyl is thicker, less flexible, and lasts 3–5 years. Always use cast vinyl for vehicle wraps.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What is the best vinyl for commercial vehicle wraps?**
A: For commercial fleet wraps, Avery Dennison MPI 1105 with DOL 1460 overlaminate is our top recommendation — 7+ year outdoor rating, excellent conformability, and clean removability. 3M IJ180-CV3 with 8518 overlaminate is an excellent alternative with 5–7 year durability.

**Q: What is the difference between cast and calendered vinyl?**
A: Cast vinyl is thinner (2 mil), more conformable, and lasts 5–7+ years. Calendered vinyl is thicker (3–4 mil), less flexible, and lasts 3–5 years. Always use cast vinyl for vehicle wraps — calendered vinyl shrinks, cracks, and lifts on curved surfaces.

## [Fleet Wrap ROI for Contractors](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/fleet-wrap-roi-for-contractors/)
> Learn how fleet wraps deliver 2,500%+ ROI for contractors in Chicago. Cost analysis, impression data, and real-world examples of fleet wrap returns.

## The Numbers: Fleet Wrap ROI Breakdown
## Why Contractors Get the Best ROI
## Fleet Wraps vs. Traditional Advertising
## Maximizing Your Fleet Wrap ROI
## Tax Benefits for Contractors
## Frequently Asked Questions
## What is the ROI of fleet wraps?
## How do fleet wraps compare to digital advertising?
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsFleet Wrap ROI for Contractors
Contractors spend thousands on Google Ads, Angi leads, and door-to-door marketing — but fleet wraps deliver the highest ROI of any advertising medium at just $0.04 per thousand impressions. Here's the math behind why wrapped contractor vehicles generate 2,500%+ returns over their lifespan.
Let's look at a real-world scenario for a Chicago contractor with 5 service vans:
Over 5 years, a fleet of 5 wrapped vans generates approximately 275 million impressions for a one-time cost of $17,500. That's a CPM of $0.06 — compared to $3.50+ for Google Display ads and $6–$12 for social media.
Contractors — HVAC technicians, plumbers, electricians, roofers, painters — have a unique advantage: their vehicles are parked in residential driveways for hours during service calls. Unlike delivery vehicles that stop briefly, a contractor's van becomes a temporary billboard in a neighborhood where every homeowner is a potential customer.
When a neighbor sees your branded van at their neighbor's house during a roof repair or furnace installation, they're making a mental note. The next time they need that service, your company name is top-of-mind — no ad spend required.
Google Local Service Ads: $25–$75 per lead in competitive trades. A single fleet wrap that generates one job per month pays for itself in the first month.
Angi & HomeAdvisor: $30–$80 per shared lead (sent to 3–4 competitors). Fleet wraps generate exclusive leads — the customer calls you directly.
Direct Mail: $0.50–$2.00 per piece with 1–2% response rates. A wrapped van delivers the same message to 30,000+ people daily with no per-impression cost.
Radio & Billboards: $1,000–$5,000/month for limited geographic targeting. Your wrapped fleet targets the exact neighborhoods where you work.
To get the highest return from your fleet wraps, follow these best practices:
1. Make your phone number the largest element. Most wrap leads come from phone calls, not website visits. Your number should be readable from 50+ feet.
2. Include your Google review rating. "4.9★ on Google — 200+ Reviews" builds instant trust and differentiates you from unmarked competitors.
3. List your core services. Don't just show your company name — tell potential customers what you do. "Heating · Cooling · 24/7 Emergency" is more effective than just "Smith HVAC."
4. Keep the design clean. The most effective wraps use high contrast, bold fonts, and limited colors. Avoid cluttering with too much text or imagery.
5. Wrap the entire fleet consistently. Brand recognition compounds with repetition. A fleet of 5 identical wraps is exponentially more effective than 5 different designs. Learn about our fleet wrap programs →

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What is the ROI of fleet wraps?**
A: Fleet wraps deliver an estimated 2,500%+ ROI over 5 years. A $3,500 van wrap generates 30,000–70,000 daily impressions at $0.04 CPM, producing millions of impressions over its 5–7 year lifespan with zero recurring cost.

**Q: How do fleet wraps compare to digital advertising?**
A: Fleet wraps cost $0.04 per 1,000 impressions vs $3.50+ CPM for Google Display, $6–$12 CPM for Facebook, and $25–$75 per lead on Google Local Service Ads. Wraps have no recurring cost after the initial investment.

## [Full Wrap vs Partial Wrap](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/full-wrap-vs-partial-wrap/)
> Compare full wraps vs partial wraps for commercial vehicles. Cost differences, coverage options, and which is right for your business fleet in Chicago.

## Cost Comparison
## What Is a Full Wrap?
## What Is a Partial Wrap?
## When to Choose a Full Wrap
## When to Choose a Partial Wrap
## Visual Impact Comparison
## Frequently Asked Questions
## What's the difference between a full wrap and a partial wrap?
## Is a partial wrap worth it?
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsFull Wrap vs Partial Wrap
Should you fully wrap your commercial vehicles or go with a partial wrap? Both options deliver strong ROI, but the right choice depends on your budget, vehicle condition, branding goals, and fleet size. Here's a comprehensive comparison to help you decide.
A full wrap covers 100% of the vehicle's painted exterior with printed vinyl. Every panel — hood, roof, sides, rear, bumpers, door handles — is wrapped. This creates the most dramatic visual impact and maximum branding real estate. Full wraps are ideal for businesses that want to make a bold statement and have a design that uses the entire vehicle as a canvas.
A partial wrap covers 40–70% of the vehicle, typically the sides and rear — the panels with the highest visibility. The hood, roof, and some panels remain in factory paint. Good partial wrap design uses the vehicle's paint color as part of the design, creating a clean, integrated look. Learn more about partial wraps →
• You want maximum visual impact and brand presence• Your vehicle paint is older, faded, or mismatched• Your design uses a different background color than the vehicle• You want to cover body imperfections• Your budget allows the investment
• You're budget-conscious but still want professional branding• Your vehicle has new, clean paint in a compatible color• You're testing fleet branding before committing to full wraps• Your design works on fewer panels (logo, phone, services)• You want to wrap a large fleet at lower per-vehicle cost
Studies show partial wraps deliver approximately 80% of the visual impact of full wraps at 50–60% of the cost. The key is design quality — a well-designed partial wrap outperforms a poorly designed full wrap every time.
The most effective partial wraps focus branding on the sides and rear — the panels most visible in traffic and while parked. A clean, bold partial wrap on a white van can look just as professional as a full wrap. See fleet wrap options →
A full wrap covers 100% of painted surfaces. A partial wrap covers 40–70%, typically sides and rear. Full wraps cost $2,800–$5,200 for vans; partial wraps cost $1,500–$2,800.
Yes — partial wraps deliver 80% of the visual impact at 50–60% of the cost. They're ideal for budget-conscious businesses, newer vehicles, and companies testing fleet branding.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What's the difference between a full wrap and a partial wrap?**
A: A full wrap covers 100% of the vehicle's painted surfaces with printed vinyl. A partial wrap covers 40–70% of the vehicle — typically the sides, rear, and hood — while leaving some factory paint visible. Full wraps cost $2,800–$5,200 for vans; partial wraps cost $1,500–$2,800.

**Q: Is a partial wrap worth it?**
A: Yes — partial wraps deliver 80% of the visual impact of a full wrap at 50–60% of the cost. They're ideal for budget-conscious businesses, newer vehicles with attractive paint, and companies that want to test fleet branding before committing to full wraps.


---
## [How Long Do Vehicle Wraps Last?](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/how-long-do-vehicle-wraps-last/)
> Vehicle wraps last 5–7 years with premium cast vinyl. Learn what affects wrap lifespan, maintenance tips, and when to replace your commercial vehicle wrap.

## Vehicle Wrap Lifespan by Material
## What Affects Vehicle Wrap Lifespan?
## How to Extend Your Wrap's Life
## Signs It's Time to Replace Your Wrap
## Frequently Asked Questions
## What is the typical lifespan of a vehicle wrap?
## How do I make my vehicle wrap last longer?
## When should I replace my vehicle wrap?
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsHow Long Do Vehicle Wraps Last?
Premium cast vinyl vehicle wraps last 5–7 years with proper care — and some last even longer. But wrap lifespan varies dramatically based on material quality, installation technique, climate, and maintenance. Here's everything you need to know about how long your wrap will last and how to maximize its lifespan in Chicago's demanding climate.
Avery Dennison MPI 1105 — Rated for 7+ years outdoor durability. Our primary film for commercial fleet wraps. With DOL 1460 overlaminate, we routinely see wraps looking great at year 5–6.
3M IJ180-CV3 — Rated for 5–7 years. Excellent Controltac adhesive technology. With 3M 8518 overlaminate, this film delivers consistent performance across Chicago's temperature extremes.
Cheap calendered vinyl — Lasts 1–3 years before cracking, shrinking, and fading. Avoid any shop using calendered vinyl for vehicle wraps. Read our vinyl comparison guide →
1. Vinyl quality. The single biggest factor. Cast vinyl outlasts calendered vinyl by 2–4 years. Premium films from Avery Dennison and 3M outlast off-brand alternatives significantly.
2. Installation quality. Poor installation causes premature failure — lifting edges, bubbles, and panel gaps. Professional installation with proper surface prep, heat application, and post-heating is essential.
3. Climate exposure. Chicago's freeze-thaw cycles, road salt, and summer UV are tough on wraps. Our material selection and installation techniques account for -10°F to 95°F+ temperature swings.
4. Vehicle storage. Garage-parked vehicles get 1–2 extra years of wrap life vs. vehicles stored outdoors 24/7. Even partial shade extends lifespan.
5. Maintenance. Regular washing prevents dirt buildup that can degrade the overlaminate. Hand washing is ideal; touchless car washes are acceptable. Avoid brush washes.
Hand wash with mild soap every 2–4 weeks. Avoid pressure washers directly on edges and seams. Rinse salt and road grime within 24 hours when possible.
Address damage immediately. Small tears and edge lifting can be repaired for $50–$150. Left unrepaired, water gets under the film and causes larger areas to fail.
Park in shade when possible. UV exposure is the #1 cause of wrap fading and overlaminate breakdown. Even intermittent shade coverage extends lifespan. Full wrap care guide →
• Visible fading or color shift across panels• Cracking or crazing in the overlaminate• Edge lifting that can't be re-sealed• Peeling around rivets, handles, or curves• Your brand or phone number has changed
Premium cast vinyl wraps last 5–7 years with proper care. Avery Dennison MPI 1105 is rated 7+ years. Lifespan depends on vinyl quality, installation, storage, climate, and maintenance.
Hand wash with mild soap, park in shade when possible, avoid brush car washes, and address any lifting edges immediately.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last?**
A: Premium cast vinyl vehicle wraps last 5–7 years with proper care. Avery Dennison MPI 1105 is rated for 7+ years. Lifespan depends on vinyl quality, installation quality, vehicle storage, climate exposure, and maintenance.

**Q: How do I make my vehicle wrap last longer?**
A: Hand wash with mild soap (no pressure washers on edges), park in shade or garage when possible, avoid automatic car washes with brushes, and address any lifting edges immediately with a heat gun or professional repair.

**Q: When should I replace my vehicle wrap?**
A: Replace your wrap when you see significant fading, cracking, edge lifting that can't be repaired, or when rebranding. Most commercial wraps should be evaluated at the 5-year mark.

## [Vehicle Wrap vs Paint Cost](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/post/vehicle-wrap-vs-paint-cost/)
> Compare vehicle wrap vs paint cost for commercial vehicles. Wraps cost $2,200–$5,200 vs $3,000–$10,000+ for paint. Wraps protect resale value and are removable.

## Cost Comparison: Wrap vs Paint
## When Wraps Win
## When Paint Wins
## The Resale Value Factor
## Frequently Asked Questions
## Is it cheaper to wrap or paint a vehicle?
## Do vehicle wraps damage paint?
## Related Articles & Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Wrap vs Paint Cost
Thinking about changing your vehicle's appearance? Whether you're rebranding a commercial fleet or customizing a personal vehicle, the wrap vs. paint decision comes down to cost, durability, flexibility, and resale value. Here's a detailed comparison to help you make the right choice.
Commercial vehicles: Wraps are the clear winner for business vehicles. You get full-color branding, logos, phone numbers, and marketing messages — something paint can't match. When it's time to rebrand or sell the vehicle, the wrap comes off cleanly, revealing the protected factory paint underneath.
Lease vehicles: Wraps are removable, making them perfect for leased fleet vehicles. Remove the wrap before the lease return and the vehicle passes inspection with factory paint intact.
Faster turnaround: A full vehicle wrap takes 2–3 days. A quality paint job takes 1–3 weeks. For commercial vehicles, less downtime means less lost revenue. Learn about fleet wrap scheduling →
Permanent color changes: If you want a permanent, single-color change on a personal vehicle and don't need graphics, a high-quality paint job may be the better option — especially for classic cars or show vehicles.
Damaged surfaces: Paint can address dents, rust, and body damage. Wraps require a clean, smooth surface — significant body damage must be repaired before wrapping.
This is where wraps have a major advantage for commercial fleet operators. A wrapped vehicle maintains its factory paint in pristine condition underneath. When you're ready to sell or trade the vehicle, remove the wrap ($300–$600) and you have a clean, factory-finish vehicle that commands higher resale value.
A repainted vehicle — especially in a non-factory color — often raises concerns for buyers and can reduce resale value by 10–20%. Full pricing details →
A quality vehicle wrap costs $2,200–$5,200 depending on vehicle size. A professional paint job costs $3,000–$10,000+. Wraps are cheaper, faster, reversible, and protect the factory paint.
No. Premium cast vinyl wraps protect factory paint from UV, scratches, and road debris. When removed properly, the original paint is preserved in better condition than unwrapped vehicles.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: Is it cheaper to wrap or paint a vehicle?**
A: A quality vehicle wrap costs $2,200–$5,200 depending on vehicle size. A professional paint job costs $3,000–$10,000+. Wraps are cheaper, faster (2–3 days vs 1–2 weeks), reversible, and protect the factory paint underneath.

**Q: Do vehicle wraps damage paint?**
A: No. Premium cast vinyl wraps actually protect factory paint from UV damage, scratches, and road debris. When removed properly, the original paint is preserved in better condition than unwrapped vehicles.

## Our Removal Process
## When to Remove a Wrap

## Will removal damage my paint?
## How long does removal take?

## Our Location
## Related Services
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsVehicle Wrap Removal Chicago
Professional vehicle wrap removal in Chicago. We safely remove full wraps, partial graphics, fleet lettering, color change wraps, and vinyl decals without damaging your factory paint. Adhesive residue cleanup included. Fleet removal pricing available. Free pickup and delivery throughout Chicagoland.
* Pricing depends on vehicle size, wrap age, and vinyl type. Older wraps and calendered vinyl take longer. Adhesive cleanup included.
Common reasons businesses request wrap removal in Chicago:
$500–$1,500 for cars, $800–$3,500 for box trucks. Pricing depends on vehicle size, vinyl age, and type. Adhesive cleanup included.
Not when done properly. We use controlled heat and slow peeling. Cast vinyl (Avery, 3M) removes cleanly. Calendered vinyl and wraps over 7 years old may require extra care.
1–2 days for a car or van. 2–3 days for a box truck. Same-day available for partial graphics and decals.
Yes. Removal + re-wrap is one of our most common services. We remove, prep, and install the new wrap in a single visit.
Yes. Lease-return wrap removal is a common service. We ensure the vehicle is returned in original condition.
Wraps past 7+ years may be more difficult to remove but we handle them regularly. Older vinyl becomes brittle and requires slower, more controlled removal. Price may be higher for aged wraps.
Yes. Fleet removal with volume pricing and free pickup. Common for rebranding, lease fleet returns, or seasonal design changes.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does wrap removal cost?**
A: Wrap removal costs $500–$1,500 for a car and $800–$3,500 for a box truck. Pricing depends on vehicle size, vinyl age, and vinyl type. Older and calendered vinyl takes longer.

**Q: Will removing a wrap damage my paint?**
A: Not when done properly. We use controlled heat and slow peeling to remove vinyl without lifting paint. Cast vinyl (Avery Dennison, 3M) removes cleanly. Calendered vinyl and wraps over 7+ years old may require more care.

**Q: How long does wrap removal take?**
A: 1–2 days for a car or van. 2–3 days for a box truck. Fleet removal is batched for efficiency. Same-day removal available for partial graphics.

**Q: Can you remove a wrap and re-wrap the same vehicle?**
A: Yes. Removal + re-wrap is one of our most common services. We remove the old wrap, prep the surface, and install the new wrap — all in one visit.

**Q: Do you remove wraps from leased vehicles?**
A: Yes. Lease-return wrap removal is a common service. We ensure the vehicle is returned in original condition with no adhesive residue.

**Q: What about adhesive residue?**
A: Residue removal is included in every wrap removal service. We use controlled heat and specialized citrus-based adhesive removers — no harsh chemicals that could damage paint.

**Q: Do you offer fleet wrap removal?**
A: Yes. We offer fleet removal pricing with free pickup. Common when companies rebrand, update designs, or return leased vehicles.

## [Fleet Wrap ROI Calculator Guide](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/roi/)
> Calculate your fleet wrap ROI. CPM data, impression counts, and cost comparisons. A wrapped cargo van generates 178 million impressions over 7 years at $0.48 CPM.
**Heading:** Fleet Wrap ROI — Guide & Calculator

## Cost Per 1,000 Impressions (CPM)
## 7-Year ROI: What One Wrapped Van Is Worth
## The Section 179 Tax Advantage
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsFleet Wrap ROI
A wrapped cargo van at $2,800–$4,500 generates approximately 178 million impressions over 7 years. No other advertising medium comes within 10x of that CPM. The numbers behind vehicle wrap marketing — sourced from OAAA and ATA data.
A vehicle wrap qualifies as an advertising expense under IRS Section 179. The full cost is deductible in the year of purchase — no depreciation schedule, no multi-year amortization. A $3,500 wrap at a 25% effective tax rate has an after-tax cost of $2,625.
- Wrap cost: $3,500 (average cargo van)
- Cost per impression: $0.00002
- After Section 179 deduction at 25% tax rate: effective cost $2,625
- Equivalent Google Ads spend for 178M impressions: $3.5 million

Schaumburg draws over 80,000 daily commuters to its office parks, retail centers, and hospitality businesses — creating massive visibility for wrapped commercial vehicles. The village's population of approximately 75,000 swells dramatically during business hours, making it one of the most trafficked suburban markets in Illinois. Companies based along Golf Road, Higgins Road, and the Woodfield business district benefit from putting their brand on wheels in an area where tens of thousands of potential customers see their vehicles daily.

The northwest suburbs have one of the highest concentrations of residential and commercial buildings in Illinois. HVAC contractors, plumbers, and electricians operating in Schaumburg wrap their fleets to build recognition across the village's residential subdivisions and commercial complexes.
Schaumburg's robust restaurant and hospitality scene extends to catering companies and food delivery services that serve corporate events, conferences, and the Schaumburg Convention Center. Wrapped delivery vehicles and food trucks create mobile brand awareness across the northwest suburbs.
Yes — we wrap fleets for companies throughout Schaumburg, including the Woodfield Mall district, Golf Road corridor, Meacham Road business parks, and all surrounding areas. Free pickup and delivery included.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do fleet wraps cost in Schaumburg?**
A: Fleet wrap pricing in Schaumburg starts at $3,150 for a cargo van full wrap and $4,700 for a box truck. Volume discounts of up to 15% are available for fleets of 10+ vehicles. Contact us for a free estimate at (312) 597-1286.

**Q: Do you offer free vehicle pickup in Schaumburg?**
A: Yes! Chicago Fleet Wraps offers free fleet pickup and delivery throughout Schaumburg and all of Chicagoland — 75+ cities. We come to your location, wrap your vehicles, and return them. No downtime hassle.

**Q: How long do vehicle wraps last in Schaumburg?**
A: We use Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl, both rated 5-7 years for outdoor durability. Our wraps come with a 2-year workmanship warranty and we have zero verified paint damage claims in 10+ years.

**Q: What types of vehicles do you wrap?**
A: We wrap cargo vans, sprinter vans, box trucks (16-26 ft), pickup trucks, transit vans, SUVs, electric vehicles (EVs), trailers, and full commercial fleets. We serve trade contractors, delivery companies, and national brands.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What types of vehicle graphics do you offer?**
A: Die-cut vinyl decals, printed graphics, vehicle lettering, DOT numbers, partial wraps, window graphics, fleet graphics packages, and yard signs.

**Q: How much do vehicle graphics cost?**
A: Die-cut decals start at $150. Vehicle lettering packages start at $250. Partial wraps start at $800. Fleet graphics packages are priced per vehicle with volume discounts.

**Q: How long do vehicle graphics last?**
A: Cut vinyl: 7–10 years. Printed graphics with UV overlaminate: 5–7 years. All graphics use premium Avery Dennison or 3M materials.

**Q: Do you design custom graphics?**
A: Yes. In-house designers create custom graphics on exact vehicle templates. You approve a digital proof before production.

**Q: Can you match graphics across a fleet?**
A: Yes. Fleet graphics programs ensure consistent branding across all vehicles. Templates are stored for future additions.

★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsSprinter Van Wraps Chicago
Professional Mercedes Sprinter van wraps in Chicago. We wrap every Sprinter configuration — standard roof, high roof, super high roof, 144" and 170" wheelbase. Full and partial wrap options with Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl rated 5–7 years outdoor. In-house design on exact Sprinter templates. 2-year workmanship warranty. Free pickup throughout Chicagoland.
The Mercedes Sprinter is the most popular commercial van in Chicago for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and delivery businesses. Its tall, flat side panels make it the ideal canvas for fleet branding — generating 30,000–70,000 daily impressions in Chicago traffic.
* All prices include design, cast vinyl, UV overlaminate, certified installation, and free pickup. Fleet discounts available for 2+ vans.
We have exact templates for every Sprinter generation and configuration:
We also wrap other cargo vans: Ford Transit, RAM ProMaster, Nissan NV, Chevy Express, and GMC Savana. See our Commercial Fleet Wraps page →
Sprinter vans have unique body lines that require specialized templates:
Sprinter vans are the fleet vehicle of choice for Chicago service businesses:
A full Sprinter van wrap costs $3,750–$6,600 depending on wheelbase and roof height. Partial wraps start at $1,200. All pricing includes design, cast vinyl, UV overlaminate, and free pickup.
Yes. High-roof and super high-roof Sprinters are our specialty. The additional surface area means more branding space and higher visibility on the road.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much does a Sprinter van wrap cost?**
A: A full Sprinter van wrap costs $4,200–$6,600 depending on wheelbase (144 vs 170) and roof height. Partial wraps start at $1,200–$2,800. All pricing includes design, cast vinyl, UV overlaminate, and free pickup.

**Q: Do you wrap high-roof Sprinters?**
A: Yes. We wrap standard roof, high roof, and super high roof Sprinter vans. High-roof models require additional vinyl and slightly longer installation but deliver significantly more visible surface area.

**Q: How long does a Sprinter wrap take?**
A: Design approval: 2–5 business days. Installation: 2–3 days for a full Sprinter wrap. Delivery back same day or next day.

**Q: Can you wrap a Sprinter with sliding door?**
A: Yes. Sliding door wraps are standard. The vinyl is cut to follow the door's track line so it opens and closes without catching or peeling.

**Q: Will a wrap damage my Sprinter's paint?**
A: No. Cast vinyl wraps are fully removable without paint damage. In fact, wraps protect the factory paint from UV, road salt, and minor scratches — preserving resale value.

**Q: Do you offer Sprinter fleet wraps?**
A: Yes. Fleet discounts: 3% for 2–4 vans, 7% for 5–9, 11% for 10–24, 15% for 25+. Same design across multiple Sprinters costs less per unit.

**Q: What Sprinter models do you wrap?**
A: All Mercedes Sprinter generations including 2019+ VS30 platform. Also Ford Transit, RAM ProMaster, Nissan NV, and Chevy Express cargo vans.

## [Avery Dennison vs 3M Vinyl Film Comparison Tool](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/vinyl-guide/)
> Side-by-side comparison of every major vinyl wrap film. Filter by use case, Chicago climate, and budget. Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 specialist.
**Heading:** Vinyl Film Comparison Tool — Avery Dennison vs 3M

## Avery Dennison MPI 1005 vs 3M Series 1080 for Chicago commercial fleet wraps — which is better?
Avery Dennison vs 3M vs calendered — side by side, filtered for your application and climate.
We'll recommend the right material for your vehicle type, use case, and budget. Free consultation.
Both are 7-year cast vinyl films rated for harsh climates including Chicago winters and road salt. Avery MPI 1005 uses air-release channels — faster install, preferred for fleet production. 3M 1080 stays repositionable longer — preferred for complex curves and color-change. Chicago Fleet Wraps is certified for both. Call (312) 597-1286.

Transform your office, retail space, restaurant, or commercial building with custom wall wraps and murals from Chicago Fleet Wraps. We've installed wall graphics on every surface type found in Chicago commercial buildings:
Our wall wrap clients span every type of commercial space in Chicago:

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: How much do wall wraps cost?**
A: Logo walls start at $500. Full wall murals range from $1,200–$5,000. Large commercial installations can run $5,000–$20,000+. Pricing depends on wall size, surface, and design complexity.

**Q: Can you wrap textured walls?**
A: Yes. We use high-tack adhesive vinyl designed for textured surfaces including drywall, concrete block, and stucco.

**Q: How long do wall wraps last?**
A: Interior: 7–10+ years. Exterior: 5–7 years with UV overlaminate. Indoor wraps see minimal wear and often last the life of the space.

**Q: Can wall wraps be removed?**
A: Yes. Quality wall vinyl is removable. Wall prep quality affects removal — well-primed walls see cleaner removal.

**Q: Do you install on-site?**
A: Yes. All wall wrap installations are done on-site at your location. We serve all of Chicagoland.

## [Wrap Warranty](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/warranty/)
> index, follow, max-snippet:-1, max-image-preview:large, max-video-preview:-1" name="robots"/><meta content="US-IL" name="geo.region"/><meta content="Chicago, Illinois" name="geo.placename"/><meta content="41.8781;-87.6298" name="geo.position"/><meta content="41.8781, -87.6298" name="ICBM"/><meta content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" name="viewport"/><title>Wrap Warranty | Chicago Fleet Wraps</title><meta content=

## Workmanship Warranty — 2 Years
## Material Warranty — 5–7 Years
## What's Not Covered
★★★★★ 4.9 · 41📞 (312) 597-1286Get Estimate Chicago Fleet WrapsWrap Warranty
Every fleet wrap from Chicago Fleet Wraps includes a 2-year workmanship warranty plus manufacturer material warranties from Avery Dennison and 3M.
- Free repair or replacement of affected panels
- Applies to all fleet wraps installed by our team
- Avery Dennison MPI 1105: 7-year outdoor vertical durability
- 3M IJ180-CV3: 5-year outdoor vertical durability
- DOL 1360 overlaminate: UV and scratch protection rated to match film life
- Covers fading, cracking, and delamination under normal conditions
- Damage from collisions, accidents, or vandalism
- Brush car wash or high-pressure washer damage
- Chemical exposure (solvents, fuel spills, harsh degreasers)
- Normal wear on high-contact areas (door handles, step rails)
